        string    `json:\\\"last_error,omitempty\\\"`\\n43\\t\\n44\\t\\t// Metrics from last check\\n45\\t\\tUploadLatencyMs   int64 `json:\\\"upload_latency_ms\\\"`\\n46\\t\\tDownloadLatencyMs int64 `json:\\\"download_latency_ms\\\"`\\n47\\t\\tDecryptVerified   bool  `json:\\\"decrypt_verified\\\"`\\n48\\t\\tHMACVerified      bool  `json:\\\"hmac_verified\\\"`\\n49\\t\\tCFCacheHit        bool  `json:\\\"cloudflare_cache_hit\\\"`\\n50\\t\\n51\\t\\t// Multipart canary state\\n52\\t\\tMultipartHealthy          Status    `json:\\\"multipart_healthy\\\"`\\n53\\t\\tMultipartLastCheck        time.Time `json:\\\"multipart_last_check\\\"`\\n54\\t\\tMultipartLastSuccess      time.Time `json:\\\"multipart_last_success\\\"`\\n55\\t\\tMultipartConsecutiveFails int       `json:\\\"multipart_consecutive_fails\\\"`\\n56\\t\\tMultipartLastError        string    `json:\\\"multipart_last_error,omitempty\\\"`\\n57\\t\\n58\\t\\t// Instance identification\\n59\\t\\tInstanceID string `json:\\\"instance_id\\\"`\\n60\\t}\\n61\\t\\n62\\t// Result represents the result of a canary check.\\n63\\ttype Result struct {\\n64\\t\\tStatus            Status    `json:\\\"status\\\"`\\n65\\t\\tLastCheck         time.Time `json:\\\"last_check\\\"`\\n66\\t\\tUploadLatencyMs   int64     `json:\\\"upload_latency_ms\\\"`\\n67\\t\\tDownloadLatencyMs int64     `json:\\\"download_latency_ms\\\"`\\n68\\t\\tDecryptVerified   bool      `json:\\\"decrypt_verified\\\"`\\n69\\t\\tHMACVerified      bool      `json:\\\"hmac_verified\\\"`\\n70\\t\\tCFCacheHit        bool      `json:\\\"cloudflare_cache_hit\\\"`\\n71\\t\\tLastError         string    `json:\\\"last_error,omitempty\\\"`\\n72\\t\\n73\\t\\t// Multipart canary result\\n74\\t\\tMultipartHealthy          Status    `json:\\\"multipart_healthy\\\"`\\n75\\t\\tMultipartLastCheck        time.Time `json:\\\"multipart_last_check\\\"`\\n76\\t\\tMultipartConsecutiveFails int       `json:\\\"multipart_consecutive_fails\\\"`\\n77\\t\\tMultipartLastError        string    `json:\\\"multipart_last_error,omitempty\\\"`\\n78\\t}\\n79\\t\\n80\\t// Monitor manages the canary integrity checks.\\n81\\ttype Monitor struct {\\n82\\t\\tbackend    backend.Backend\\n83\\t\\tbucket     string\\n84\\t\\tmek        []byte\\n85\\t\\tblockSize  int\\n86\\t\\tinstanceID string\\n87\\t\\n88\\t\\tstate CanaryState\\n89\\t\\n90\\t\\t// Configuration\\n91\\t\\tinterval          time.Duration\\n92\\t\\tcanarySize        int\\n93\\t\\tmaxRetries        int\\n94\\t\\tretryDelay        time.Duration\\n95\\t\\tmultipartInterval time.Duration\\n96\\t\\tmultipartSize     int\\n97\\t\\n98\\t\\t// Control\\n99\\t\\tstopCh chan struct{}\\n100\\t\\tdoneCh chan struct{}\\n101\\t}\\n102\\t\\n103\\t// Config holds configuration for the canary monitor.\\n104\\ttype Config struct {\\n105\\t\\tBackend          backend.Backend\\n106\\t\\tBucket           string\\n107\\t\\tMEK              []byte\\n108\\t\\tBlockSize        int\\n109\\t\\tInstanceID       string\\n110\\t\\tInterval         time.Duration // Check interval (default 5 minutes)\\n111\\t\\tCanarySize       int           // Size of canary content (default 1024 bytes)\\n112\\t\\tMaxRetries       int           // Max retries on failure (default 3)\\n113\\t\\tRetryDelay       time.Duration // Delay between retries (default 10s)\\n114\\t\\tMultipartInterval time.Duration // Multipart check interval (default 1 hour)\\n115\\t\\tMultipartSize     int           // Size of multipart canary (default 6MB)\\n116\\t}\\n117\\t\\n118\\t// NewMonitor creates a new canary monitor.\\n119\\tfunc NewMonitor(cfg Config) *Monitor {\\n120\\t\\tif cfg.Interval == 0 {\\n121\\t\\t\\tcfg.Interval = 5 * time.Minute\\n122\\t\\t}\\n123\\t\\tif cfg.CanarySize == 0 {\\n124\\t\\t\\tcfg.CanarySize = 1024\\n125\\t\\t}\\n126\\t\\tif cfg.MaxRetries == 0 {\\n127\\t\\t\\tcfg.MaxRetries = 3\\n128\\t\\t}\\n129\\t\\tif cfg.RetryDelay == 0 {\\n130\\t\\t\\tcfg.RetryDelay = 10 * time.Second\\n131\\t\\t}\\n132\\t\\tif cfg.MultipartInterval == 0 {\\n133\\t\\t\\tcfg.MultipartInterval = 1 * time.Hour\\n134\\t\\t}\\n135\\t\\tif cfg.MultipartSize == 0 {\\n136\\t\\t\\tcfg.MultipartSize = 6 * 1024 * 1024 // 6MB (above 5MB threshold)\\n137\\t\\t}\\n138\\t\\n139\\t\\tinstanceID := cfg.InstanceID\\n140\\t\\tif instanceID == \\\"\\\" {\\n141\\t\\t\\tinstanceID, _ = os.Hostname()\\n142\\t\\t\\tif instanceID == \\\"\\\" {\\n143\\t\\t\\t\\t// Generate random ID\\n144\\t\\t\\t\\tb := make([]byte, 8)\\n145\\t\\t\\t\\trand.Read(b)\\n146\\t\\t\\t\\tinstanceID = hex.EncodeToString(b)\\n147\\t\\t\\t}\\n148\\t\\t}\\n149\\t\\n150\\t\\treturn &Monitor{\\n151\\t\\t\\tbackend:           cfg.Backend,\\n152\\t\\t\\tbucket:            cfg.Bucket,\\n153\\t\\t\\tmek:               cfg.MEK,\\n154\\t\\t\\tblockSize:         cfg.BlockSize,\\n155\\t\\t\\tinstanceID:        instanceID,\\n156\\t\\t\\tinterval:          cfg.Interval,\\n157\\t\\t\\tcanarySize:        cfg.CanarySize,\\n158\\t\\t\\tmaxRetries:        cfg.MaxRetries,\\n159\\t\\t\\tretryDelay:        cfg.RetryDelay,\\n160\\t\\t\\tmultipartInterval: cfg.MultipartInterval,\\n161\\t\\t\\tmultipartSize:     cfg.MultipartSize,\\n162\\t\\t\\tstopCh:            make(chan struct{}),\\n163\\t\\t\\tdoneCh:            make(chan struct{}),\\n164\\t\\t\\tstate: CanaryState{\\n165\\t\\t\\t\\tStatus:           StatusUnknown,\\n166\\t\\t\\t\\tMultipartHealthy: StatusUnknown,\\n167\\t\\t\\t\\tInstanceID:       instanceID,\\n168\\t\\t\\t},\\n169\\t\\t}\\n170\\t}\\n171\\t\\n172\\t// Start begins the periodic canary checks.\\n173\\t// It runs an initial check immediately, then periodically.\\n174\\tfunc (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) {\\n175\\t\\tgo func() {\\n176\\t\\t\\tdefer close(m.doneCh)\\n177\\t\\n178\\t\\t\\t// Initial checks\\n179\\t\\t\\tm.runCheck(ctx)\\n180\\t\\t\\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\\n181\\t\\n182\\t\\t\\tticker := time.NewTicker(m.interval)\\n183\\t\\t\\tmultipartTicker := time.NewTicker(m.multipartInterval)\\n184\\t\\t\\tdefer ticker.Stop()\\n185\\t\\t\\tdefer multipartTicker.Stop()\\n186\\t\\n187\\t\\t\\tfor {\\n188\\t\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n189\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-m.stopCh:\\n190\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n191\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-ctx.Done():\\n192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n193\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-ticker.C:\\n194\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tm.runCheck(ctx)\\n195\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-multipartTicker.C:\\n196\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\\n197\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n198\\t\\t\\t}\\n199\\t\\t}()\\n200\\t}\\n201\\t\\n202\\t// Stop stops the canary monitor.\\n203\\tfunc (m *Monitor) Stop() {\\n204\\t\\tclose(m.stopCh)\\n205\\t\\t<-m.doneCh\\n206\\t}\\n207\\t\\n208\\t// runCheck performs a single canary check with retries.\\n209\\tfunc (m *Monitor) runCheck(ctx context.Context) {\\n210\\t\\tvar lastErr error\\n211\\t\\n212\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryChecks()\\n213\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\\n214\\t\\n215\\t\\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\\n216\\t\\t\\tif attempt > 0 {\\n217\\t\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n218\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\\n219\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-ctx.Done():\\n220\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n221\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n222\\t\\t\\t}\\n223\\t\\n224\\t\\t\\tresult, err := m.check(ctx)\\n225\\t\\t\\tif err == nil {\\n226\\t\\t\\t\\tm.updateStateSuccess(result)\\n227\\t\\t\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(\\\"\\\")\\n228\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n229\\t\\t\\t}\\n230\\t\\t\\tlastErr = err\\n231\\t\\t}\\n232\\t\\n233\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryFailures()\\n234\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\\n235\\t\\tm.updateStateFailure(lastErr)\\n236\\t}\\n237\\t\\n238\\t// runMultipartCheck performs a single multipart canary check with retries.\\n239\\tfunc (m *Monitor) runMultipartCheck(ctx context.Context) {\\n240\\t\\tvar lastErr error\\n241\\t\\n242\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryChecks()\\n243\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\\n244\\t\\n245\\t\\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\\n246\\t\\t\\tif attempt > 0 {\\n247\\t\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n248\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\\n249\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-ctx.Done():\\n250\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n251\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n252\\t\\t\\t}\\n253\\t\\n254\\t\\t\\tresult, err := m.checkMultipart(ctx)\\n255\\t\\t\\tif err == nil {\\n256\\t\\t\\t\\tm.updateMultipartStateSuccess(result)\\n257\\t\\t\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(\\\"\\\")\\n258\\t\\t\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(true)\\n259\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n260\\t\\t\\t}\\n261\\t\\t\\tlastErr = err\\n262\\t\\t}\\n263\\t\\n264\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryFailures()\\n265\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\\n266\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(false)\\n267\\t\\tm.updateMultipartStateFailure(lastErr)\\n268\\t}\\n269\\t\\n270\\t// check performs a single canary integrity check.\\n271\\tfunc (m *Monitor) check(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\\n272\\t\\tresult := &Result{\\n273\\t\\t\\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\\n274\\t\\t}\\n275\\t\\n276\\t\\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\\n277\\t\\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\\n278\\t\\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.canarySize)\\n279\\t\\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\\n280\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate canary content: %w\\\", err)\\n281\\t\\t}\\n282\\t\\n283\\t\\t// Embed timestamp for verification\\n284\\t\\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\\n285\\t\\n286\\t\\t// Generate unique key for this canary\\n287\\t\\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\\\".armor/canary/%s/%d\\\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\\n288\\t\\n289\\t\\t// Generate DEK and IV\\n290\\t\\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\\n291\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n292\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n293\\t\\t}\\n294\\t\\n295\\t\\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\\n296\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n297\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate IV: %w\\\", err)\\n298\\t\\t}\\n299\\t\\n300\\t\\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\\n301\\t\\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\\n302\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n303\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n304\\t\\t}\\n305\\t\\n306\\t\\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\\n307\\t\\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\\n308\\t\\n309\\t\\t// Create envelope header\\n310\\t\\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\\n311\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n312\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create header: %w\\\", err)\\n313\\t\\t}\\n314\\t\\n315\\t\\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\\n316\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n317\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode header: %w\\\", err)\\n318\\t\\t}\\n319\\t\\n320\\t\\t// Encrypt\\n321\\t\\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\\n322\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n323\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create encryptor: %w\\\", err)\\n324\\t\\t}\\n325\\t\\n326\\t\\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\\n327\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n328\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encrypt: %w\\\", err)\\n329\\t\\t}\\n330\\t\\n331\\t\\t// Build envelope\\n332\\t\\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\\n333\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\\n334\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\\n335\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\\n336\\t\\n337\\t\\t// Compute ETag\\n338\\t\\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\\n339\\t\\n340\\t\\t// Build metadata\\n341\\t\\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\\n342\\t\\t\\tVersion:       1,\\n343\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\\n344\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\\n345\\t\\t\\tContentType:   \\\"application/octet-stream\\\",\\n346\\t\\t\\tIV:            iv,\\n347\\t\\t\\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\\n348\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\\n349\\t\\t\\tETag:          etag,\\n350\\t\\t}).ToMetadata()\\n351\\t\\n352\\t\\t// Upload to B2 (direct, not through Cloudflare)\\n353\\t\\tuploadStart := time.Now()\\n354\\t\\tif err := m.backend.Put(ctx, m.bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\\n355\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to upload canary: %w\\\", err)\\n356\\t\\t}\\n357\\t\\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\\n358\\t\\n359\\t\\t// Download through Cloudflare (via GetRangeWithHeaders to capture CF-Cache-Status)\\n360\\t\\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\\n361\\t\\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\\n362\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n363\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to download canary: %w\\\", err)\\n364\\t\\t}\\n365\\t\\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\\n366\\t\\tbody.Close()\\n367\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n368\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to read downloaded canary: %w\\\", err)\\n369\\t\\t}\\n370\\t\\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\\n371\\t\\n372\\t\\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\\n373\\t\\t// CF-Cache-Status values: HIT, MISS, EXPIRED, STALE, BYPASS, REVALIDATED, UPDATING, IGNORED\\n374\\t\\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\\\"CF-Cache-Status\\\"]; ok {\\n375\\t\\t\\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \\\"HIT\\\" || cfStatus == \\\"STALE\\\" || cfStatus == \\\"REVALIDATED\\\")\\n376\\t\\t}\\n377\\t\\n378\\t\\t// Parse header\\n379\\t\\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\\n380\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n381\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to decode header: %w\\\", err)\\n382\\t\\t}\\n383\\t\\n384\\t\\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\\n385\\t\\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\\n386\\t\\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\\n387\\t\\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\\n388\\t\\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\\n389\\t\\n390\\t\\tif hmacEnd > int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\\n391\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\\\")\\n392\\t\\t}\\n393\\t\\n394\\t\\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\\n395\\t\\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\\n396\\t\\n397\\t\\t// Unwrap DEK\\n398\\t\\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\\n399\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n400\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n401\\t\\t}\\n402\\t\\n403\\t\\t// Create decryptor\\n404\\t\\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, m.blockSize)\\n405\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n406\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create decryptor: %w\\\", err)\\n407\\t\\t}\\n408\\t\\n409\\t\\t// Verify HMACs\\n410\\t\\tif err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\\n411\\t\\t\\tresult.HMACVerified = false\\n412\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"HMAC verification failed: %w\\\", err)\\n413\\t\\t}\\n414\\t\\tresult.HMACVerified = true\\n415\\t\\n416\\t\\t// Decrypt\\n417\\t\\tdecrypted, err := decryptor.Decrypt(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC)\\n418\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n419\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to decrypt: %w\\\", err)\\n420\\t\\t}\\n421\\t\\n422\\t\\t// Verify decrypted content matches original\\n423\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\\n424\\t\\t\\tresult.DecryptVerified = false\\n425\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"decrypted content does not match original\\\")\\n426\\t\\t}\\n427\\t\\tresult.DecryptVerified = true\\n428\\t\\n429\\t\\t// Verify plaintext SHA\\n430\\t\\tif err := downloadedHeader.VerifyPlaintextSHA(decrypted); err != nil {\\n431\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"plaintext SHA verification failed: %w\\\", err)\\n432\\t\\t}\\n433\\t\\n434\\t\\t// Clean up canary (best effort)\\n435\\t\\tgo func() {\\n436\\t\\t\\tcleanupCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)\\n437\\t\\t\\tdefer cancel()\\n438\\t\\t\\tm.backend.Delete(cleanupCtx, m.bucket, key)\\n439\\t\\t}()\\n440\\t\\n441\\t\\tresult.Status = StatusHealthy\\n442\\t\\n443\\t\\treturn result, nil\\n444\\t}\\n445\\t\\n446\\t// checkMultipart performs a multipart canary check to exercise the multipart upload path.\\n447\\tfunc (m *Monitor) checkMultipart(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\\n448\\t\\tresult := &Result{\\n449\\t\\t\\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\\n450\\t\\t}\\n451\\t\\n452\\t\\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\\n453\\t\\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\\n454\\t\\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.multipartSize)\\n455\\t\\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\\n456\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate multipart canary content: %w\\\", err)\\n457\\t\\t}\\n458\\t\\n459\\t\\t// Embed timestamp for verification\\n460\\t\\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\\n461\\t\\n462\\t\\t// Generate unique key for this canary\\n463\\t\\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\\\".armor/canary-multipart/%s/%d\\\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\\n464\\t\\n465\\t\\t// Generate DEK and IV\\n466\\t\\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\\n467\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n468\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n469\\t\\t}\\n470\\t\\n471\\t\\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\\n472\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n473\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate IV: %w\\\", err)\\n474\\t\\t}\\n475\\t\\n476\\t\\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\\n477\\t\\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\\n478\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n479\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n480\\t\\t}\\n481\\t\\n482\\t\\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\\n483\\t\\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\\n484\\t\\n485\\t\\t// Create envelope header\\n486\\t\\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\\n487\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n488\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create header: %w\\\", err)\\n489\\t\\t}\\n490\\t\\n491\\t\\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\\n492\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n493\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode header: %w\\\", err)\\n494\\t\\t}\\n495\\t\\n496\\t\\t// Encrypt\\n497\\t\\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\\n498\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n499\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create encryptor: %w\\\", err)\\n500\\t\\t}\\n501\\t\\n502\\t\\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\\n503\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n504\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encrypt: %w\\\", err)\\n505\\t\\t}\\n506\\t\\n507\\t\\t// Build envelope\\n508\\t\\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\\n509\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\\n510\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\\n511\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\\n512\\t\\n513\\t\\t// Compute ETag\\n514\\t\\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\\n515\\t\\n516\\t\\t// Build metadata\\n517\\t\\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\\n518\\t\\t\\tVersion:       1,\\n519\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\\n520\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\\n521\\t\\t\\tContentType:   \\\"application/octet-stream\\\",\\n522\\t\\t\\tIV:            iv,\\n523\\t\\t\\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\\n524\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\\n525\\t\\t\\tETag:          etag,\\n526\\t\\t}).ToMetadata()\\n527\\t\\n528\\t\\t// Upload via multipart API (exercises create/upload/complete path)\\n529\\t\\tuploadStart := time.Now()\\n530\\t\\n531\\t\\t// Step 1: Create multipart upload\\n532\\t\\tuploadID, err := m.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, meta)\\n533\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n534\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create multipart upload: %w\\\", err)\\n535\\t\\t}\\n536\\t\\n537\\t\\t// Step 2: Upload parts (split into 2MB parts for reasonable part count)\\n538\\t\\tconst partSize = 2 * 1024 * 1024 // 2MB parts\\n539\\t\\tvar parts []backend.CompletedPart\\n540\\t\\tpartNum := int32(1)\\n541\\t\\n542\\t\\tfor offset := 0; offset < len(envelope); offset += partSize {\\n543\\t\\t\\tend := offset + partSize\\n544\\t\\t\\tif end > len(envelope) {\\n545\\t\\t\\t\\tend = len(envelope)\\n546\\t\\t\\t}\\n547\\t\\n548\\t\\t\\tpartData := envelope[offset:end]\\n549\\t\\t\\tpartETag, err := m.backend.UploadPart(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, partNum, bytes.NewReader(partData), int64(len(partData)))\\n550\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n551\\t\\t\\t\\t// Abort on failure\\n552\\t\\t\\t\\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, Package backend provides low-level multipart upload operations for S3-compatible storage.\\n2\\t//\\n3\\t// Multipart Upload Primitives\\n4\\t//\\n5\\t// This package provides low-level multipart are:\\n9\\t//\\n10\\t//   CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, metadata) -> uploadID\\n11\\t//     Initiates a multipart upload and returns an upload ID that must be used for\\n12\\t// Creates a helper for a specific bucket.\\n33\\t//\\n34\\t//   helper.CreateUpload(ctx, key, metadata) -> uploadInfo\\n35\\t//     Creates a new multipart upload.\\n36\\t//\\n37\\t// bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\\n245\\t\\tm.mu.Lock()\\n246\\t\\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\\n247\\t\\n248\\t\\t// Read the part data\\n249\\t\\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\\n250\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n251\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to read part data: %w\\\", err)\\n252\\t\\t}\\n253\\t\\n254\\t\\t// Store the part data\\n255\\t\\tif _, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]; !exists {\\n256\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"upload ID %s does not exist\\\", sha256.Sum256(data), partNumber), nil\\n262\\t}\\n263\\t\\n264\\tfunc (m *mockBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\\n265\\t\\tm.mu.Lock()\\n266\\t\\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\\n267\\t\\n268\\t\\t// Check if upload exists\\n269\\t\\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\\n270\\t\\tif !exists {\\n271\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"upload ID %s does not exist\\\", uploadID)\\n272\\t\\t}\\n273\\t\\n274\\t\\t// Combine all parts in order\\n275\\t\\tvar combined []byte\\n276\\t\\tfor _, part := range parts {\\n277\\t\\t\\tpartData, ok := partsData[part.PartNumber]\\n278\\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n279\\t\\t\\t\\t// Clean up and return error\\n280\\t\\t\\t\\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\\n281\\t\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"part %d not found\\\", part.PartNumber)\\n282\\t\\t\\t}\\n283\\t\\t\\tcombined = append(combined, partData...)\\n284\\t\\t}\\n285\\t\\n286\\t\\t// Store the combined object\\n287\\t\\tobjectKey := bucket + \\\"/\\\" + key\\n288\\t\\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\\n289\\t\\n290\\t\\t// Move {\\n293\\t\\t\\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\\n294\\t\\t\\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\\n295\\t\\t}\\n296\\t\\n297\\t\\t// Clean up multipart data\\n298\\t\\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\\n299\\t\\n300\\t\\t// Return final ETag\\n301\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"final-etag-%x\\\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\\n302\\t}\\n303\\t\\n304\\tfunc (m *mockBackend) make([]byte, 32)\\n428\\t\\trand.Read(mek)\\n429\\t\\n430\\t\\tcfg := Config{\\n431\\t\\t\\tBackend:    mb,\\n432\\t\\t\\tBucket:     \\\"test-bucket\\\",\\n433\\t\\t\\tMEK: {\\n463\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"expected canary to be cleaned up, found %d objects\\\", count)\\n464\\t\\t}\\n465\\t}\\n466\\t\\n467\\t// TestMonitorGetStatus tests the status retrieval.\\n468\\tfunc TestMonitorGetStatus(t *testing.T) {\\n469\\t\\tmb := newMockBackend()\\n470\\t\\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\\n471\\t\\trand.Read(mek)\\n472\\t\\n473\\t\\tcfg := Config{\\n474\\t\\t\\tBackend: newMockBackend()\\n503\\t\\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\\n504\\t\\trand.Read(mek)\\n505\\t\\n506\\t\\tcfg := Config{\\n507\\t\\t\\tBackend:    mb,\\n508\\t\\t\\tBucket:     \\\"test-bucket\\\",\\n509\\t\\t\\tMEK:        mek,\\n510\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:  65536,\\n511\\t\\t\\tInstanceID: \\\"test-instance\\\",\\n512\\t\\t}\\n513\\t\\n514\\t\\tm := !m.IsHealthy() {\\n527\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"expected IsHealthy to be true after successful check\\\")\\n528\\t\\t}\\n529\\t}\\n530\\t\\n531\\t// TestMonitorWrongMEK tests that using a different MEK to decrypt data fails.\\n532\\t// This simulates a scenario where data was encrypted with one MEK but someone\\n533\\t// tries to decrypt with a different MEK.\\n534\\tfunc TestMonitorWrongMEK(t *testing.T) {\\n535\\t\\tmb := newMockBackend()\\n536\\t\\n537\\t\\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\\n538\\t\\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\\n539\\t\\trand.Read(correctMEK)\\n540\\t\\n541\\t\\twrongMEK := make([]byte, 32)\\n542\\t\\trand.Read(wrongMEK)\\n543\\t\\n544\\t\\t// Create test content\\n545\\t\\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, 100)\\n546\\t\\trand.Read(canaryContent)\\n547\\t\\n548\\t\\t// Generate DEK and IV\\n549\\t\\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\\n550\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n551\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to generate DEK: %v\\\", err)\\n552\\t\\t}\\n553\\t\\n554\\t\\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\\n555\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n556\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to generate IV: %v\\\", err)\\n557\\t\\t}\\n558\\t\\n559\\t\\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\\n560\\t\\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\\n561\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n562\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to wrap DEK: %v\\\", err)\\n563\\t\\t}\\n564\\t\\n565\\t\\t// Encrypt with the DEK\\n566\\t\\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\\n567\\t\\n568\\t\\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), 65536, plaintextSHA)\\n569\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n570\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to create header: %v\\\", err)\\n571\\t\\t}\\n572\\t\\n573\\t\\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\\n574\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n575\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to encode header: %v\\\", err)\\n576\\t\\t}\\n577\\t\\n578\\t\\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, 65536)\\n579\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n580\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to create encryptor: %v\\\", err)\\n581\\t\\t}\\n582\\t\\n583\\t\\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\\n584\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n585\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to encrypt: %v\\\", err)\\n586\\t\\t}\\n587\\t\\n588\\t\\t// Build envelope\\n589\\t\\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\\n590\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\\n591\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\\n592\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\\n593\\t\\n594\\t\\t// Compute ETag\\n595\\t\\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\\n596\\t\\n597\\t\\t// Build metadata with the wrapped DEK (wrapped with CORRECT MEK)\\n598\\t\\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\\n599\\t\\t\\tVersion:       1,\\n600\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     65536,\\n601\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\\n602\\t\\t\\tContentType:   \\\"application/octet-stream\\\",\\n603\\t\\t\\tIV:            iv,\\n604\\t\\t\\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\\n605\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\\n606\\t\\t\\tETag:          etag,\\n607\\t\\t}).ToMetadata()\\n608\\t\\n609\\t\\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\\n610\\t\\tkey := \\\".armor/canary/test-instance/pre-encrypted\\\"\\n611\\t\\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \\\"test-bucket\\\", key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta)\\n612\\t\\n613\\t\\t// Now try to unwrap the DEK with the WRONG MEK\\n614\\t\\t// This should fail because the wrapped DEK was encrypted with the correct MEK\\n615\\t\\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(wrongMEK, wrappedDEK)\\n616\\t\\tif err == nil {\\n617\\t\\t\\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\\n618\\t\\t\\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\\n619\\t\\t\\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, 65536)\\n620\\t\\t\\tif decryptorErr != nil {\\n621\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Logf(\\\"decryptor creation failed as expected: %v\\\", decryptorErr)\\n622\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n623\\t\\t\\t}\\n624\\t\\n625\\t\\t\\t// Try to verify HMACs - should fail because the DEK is wrong\\n626\\t\\t\\tverifyErr := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(encrypted, hmacTable)\\n627\\t\\t\\tif verifyErr == nil {\\n628\\t\\t\\t\\t// If HMAC verification somehow passed, try decryption and content check\\n629\\t\\t\\t\\tdecrypted, decryptErr := decryptor.Decrypt(encrypted, hmacTable)\\n630\\t\\t\\t\\tif decryptErr == nil {\\n631\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\\n632\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"expected decryption with wrong MEK to fail or produce garbage, but content matched\\\")\\n633\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n634\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Content didn't match - that's the expected failure\\n635\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Logf(\\\"decryption produced wrong content (expected): got %d bytes, expected %d bytes\\\", len(decrypted), len(canaryContent))\\n636\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n637\\t\\t\\t}\\n638\\t\\t} else {\\n639\\t\\t\\t// Unwrap failed - that's the expected behavior\\n640\\t\\t\\tt.Logf(\\\"DEK unwrap failed as expected with wrong MEK: %v\\\", err)\\n641\\t\\t}\\n642\\t}\\n643\\t\\n644\\t// TestMonitorStartStop tests starting and stopping the monitor.\\n645\\tfunc TestMonitorStartStop(t *testing.T) {\\n646\\t\\tmb := Backend-level tests and the 1KB canary were\\n9\\t// structurally blind to it. These tests exercise the full HTTP handler path\\n10\\t// with a recording backend so a routing regression fails loudly.\\n11\\t\\n12\\timport (\\n13\\t\\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n14\\t\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n15\\t\\t\\\"crypto/rand\\\"\\n16\\t\\t\\\"encoding/xml\\\"\\n17\\t\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n18\\t\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n19\\t\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n20\\t\\t\\\"net/http/httptest\\\"\\n21\\t\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n22\\t\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n23\\t\\t\\\"testing\\\"\\n24\\t\\n25\\t\\t\\\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/backend\\\"\\n26\\t\\t\\\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/config\\\"\\n27\\t\\t\\\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/keymanager\\\"\\n28\\t\\t\\\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/server/handlers\\\"\\n29\\t)\\n30\\t\\n31\\t// recordingBackend wraps mockBackend, records which backend methods are\\n32\\t// invoked per object key, and implements a real in-memory multipart\\n33\\t// store: parts are kept per uploadID and concatenated (in the part order\\n34\\t// given to CompleteMultipartUpload) into the object map on completion —\\n35\\t// mirroring what B2 does on b2_finish_large_file.\\n36\\ttype recordingBackend struct {\\n37\\t\\t*mockBackend\\n38\\t\\n39\\t\\trmu      sync.Mutex\\n40\\t\\tputKeys  []string // keys passed to Put (includes state/sidecar writes)\\n41\\t\\tpartKeys []string // keys passed to UploadPart\\n42\\t\\tuploads  map[string]map[int32][]byte\\n43\\t\\tnextID   int\\n44\\t}\\n45\\t\\n46\\tfunc newRecordingBackend() *recordingBackend {\\n47\\t\\treturn &recordingBackend{\\n48\\t\\t\\tmockBackend: newMockBackend(),\\n49\\t\\t\\tuploads:     make(map[string]map[int32][]byte),\\n50\\t\\t}\\n51\\t}\\n52\\t\\n53\\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) Put(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, body io.Reader, size int64, meta map[string]string) error {\\n54\\t\\tr.rmu.Lock()\\n55\\t\\tr.putKeys = append(r.putKeys, bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key)\\n56\\t\\tr.rmu.Unlock()\\n57\\t\\treturn r.mockBackend.Put(ctx, bucket, key, body, size, meta)\\n58\\t}\\n59\\t\\n60\\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\\n61\\t\\tr.rmu.Lock()\\n62\\t\\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\\n63\\t\\tr.nextID++\\n64\\t\\tid := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"upload-%d\\\", r.nextID)\\n65\\t\\tr.uploads[id] = make(map[int32][]byte)\\n66\\t\\treturn id, nil\\n67\\t}\\n68\\t\\n69\\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\\n70\\t\\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\\n71\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n72\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", err\\n73\\t\\t}\\n74\\t\\tr.rmu.Lock()\\n75\\t\\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\\n76\\t\\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\\n77\\t\\tif !ok {\\n78\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"no such upload: %s\\\", uploadID)\\n79\\t\\t}\\n80\\t\\tparts[partNumber] = data\\n81\\t\\tr.partKeys = append(r.partKeys, bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key)\\n82\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"etag-part-%d\\\", partNumber), nil\\n83\\t}\\n84\\t\\n85\\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, completed []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\\n86\\t\\tr.rmu.Lock()\\n87\\t\\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\\n88\\t\\tif !ok {\\n89\\t\\t\\tr.rmu.Unlock()\\n90\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"no such upload: %s\\\", uploadID)\\n91\\t\\t}\\n92\\t\\tvar assembled []byte\\n93\\t\\tfor _, p := range completed {\\n94\\t\\t\\tdata, ok := parts[p.PartNumber]\\n95\\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n96\\t\\t\\t\\tr.rmu.Unlock()\\n97\\t\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"missing part %d\\\", p.PartNumber)\\n98\\t\\t\\t}\\n99\\t\\t\\tassembled = append(assembled, data...)\\n100\\t\\t}\\n101\\t\\tdelete(r.uploads, uploadID)\\n102\\t\\tr.rmu.Unlock()\\n103\\t\\n104\\t\\t// Store assembled object exactly as B2 would, preserving no metadata —\\n105\\t\\t// the handler applies ARMOR metadata afterwards via Copy.\\n106\\t\\tr.mu.Lock()\\n107\\t\\tr.objects[bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key] = assembled\\n108\\t\\tif _, ok := r.meta[bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key]; !ok {\\n109\\t\\t\\tr.meta[bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key] = map[string]string{}\\n110\\t\\t}\\n111\\t\\tr.mu.Unlock()\\n112\\t\\treturn \\\"etag-assembled\\\", nil\\n113\\t}\\n114\\t\\n115\\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\\n116\\t\\tr.rmu.Lock()\\n117\\t\\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\\n118\\t\\tdelete(r.uploads, uploadID)\\n119\\t\\treturn nil\\n120\\t}\\n121\\t\\n122\\t// objectPutCount returns how many times Put was called with exactly this key.\\n123\\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) objectPutCount(bucket, key string) int {\\n124\\t\\tr.rmu.Lock()\\n125\\t\\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\\n126\\t\\tn := 0\\n127\\t\\tfor _, k := range r.putKeys {\\n128\\t\\t\\tif k == bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key {\\n129\\t\\t\\t\\tn++\\n130\\t\\t\\t}\\n131\\t\\t}\\n132\\t\\treturn n\\n133\\t}\\n134\\t\\n135\\tfunc recordingTestSetup(t *testing.T) (*config.Config, *recordingBackend, *handlers.Handlers) {\\n136\\t\\tt.Helper()\\n137\\t\\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\\n138\\t\\tif _, err := rand.Read(mek); err != nil {\\n139\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to generate MEK: %v\\\", err)\\n140\\t\\t}\\n141\\t\\tcfg := &config.Config{\\n142\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     65536,\\n143\\t\\t\\tAuthAccessKey: \\\"test-access-key\\\",\\n144\\t\\t\\tAuthSecretKey: \\\"test-secret-key\\\",\\n145\\t\\t}\\n146\\t\\trb := newRecordingBackend()\\n147\\t\\tcache := backend.NewMetadataCache(1000, 300)\\n148\\t\\tfooterCache := backend.NewFooterCache(1000, 300)\\n149\\t\\tkm, err := keymanager.New(mek, nil, nil)\\n150\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n151\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to create key manager: %v\\\", err)\\n152\\t\\t}\\n153\\t\\th := handlers.New(cfg, rb, cache, footerCache, km, nil)\\n154\\t\\treturn cfg, rb, h\\n155\\t}\\n156\\t\\n157\\tfunc initiateMultipart(t *testing.T, h *handlers.Handlers, bucket, key string) string {\\n158\\t\\tt.Helper()\\n159\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?uploads\\\", bucket, key), nil)\\n160\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/octet-stream\\\")\\n161\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n162\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n163\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n164\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n165\\t\\t}\\n166\\t\\tvar result struct {\\n167\\t\\t\\tUploadID string `xml:\\\"UploadId\\\"`\\n168\\t\\t}\\n169\\t\\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &result); err != nil {\\n170\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to parse initiate response: %v\\\", err)\\n171\\t\\t}\\n172\\t\\tif result.UploadID == \\\"\\\" {\\n173\\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(\\\"empty UploadId in initiate response\\\")\\n174\\t\\t}\\n175\\t\\treturn result.UploadID\\n176\\t}\\n177\\t\\n178\\tfunc uploadPart(t *testing.T, h *handlers.Handlers, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int, body []byte) string {\\n179\\t\\tt.Helper()\\n180\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?partNumber=%d&uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, partNumber, uploadID)\\n181\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(body))\\n182\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n183\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n184\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n185\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"UploadPart %d failed: status %d: %s\\\", partNumber, w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n186\\t\\t}\\n187\\t\\tetag := w.Header().Get(\\\"ETag\\\")\\n188\\t\\tif etag == \\\"\\\" {\\n189\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"UploadPart %d returned no ETag\\\", partNumber)\\n190\\t\\t}\\n191\\t\\treturn etag\\n192\\t}\\n193\\t\\n194\\tfunc completeMultipart(t *testing.T, h *handlers.Handlers, bucket, key, uploadID string, etags []string) {\\n195\\t\\tt.Helper()\\n196\\t\\tvar xmlBody bytes.Buffer\\n197\\t\\txmlBody.WriteString(\\\"<CompleteMultipartUpload>\\\")\\n198\\t\\tfor i, etag := range etags {\\n199\\t\\t\\tfmt.Fprintf(&xmlBody, \\\"<Part><PartNumber>%d</PartNumber><ETag>%s</ETag></Part>\\\", i+1, etag)\\n200\\t\\t}\\n201\\t\\txmlBody.WriteString(\\\"</CompleteMultipartUpload>\\\")\\n202\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, uploadID)\\n203\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, url, &xmlBody)\\n204\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n205\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n206\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n207\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"CompleteMultipartUpload failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n208\\t\\t}\\n209\\t}\\n210\\t\\n211\\t// TestUploadPartRoutingNeverFallsThroughToPut is the ADR-002 regression\\n212\\t// tripwire. The 2026-06 bug routed `PUT ?partNumber&uploadId` to PutObject;\\n213\\t// this asserts the part request reaches UploadPart and that Put is never\\n214\\t// invoked for the object key itself (state/sidecar writes use other keys).\\n215\\tfunc TestUploadPartRoutingNeverFallsThroughToPut(t *testing.T) {\\n216\\t\\t_, rb, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n217\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"routing-tripwire.dat\\\"\\n218\\t\\n219\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n220\\t\\tpart := make([]byte, 2*65536) // two full blocks\\n221\\t\\tfor i := range part {\\n222\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = byte(i % 251)\\n223\\t\\t}\\n224\\t\\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part)\\n225\\t\\n226\\t\\trb.rmu.Lock()\\n227\\t\\tpartCalls := len(rb.partKeys)\\n228\\t\\trb.rmu.Unlock()\\n229\\t\\tif partCalls == 0 {\\n230\\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(\\\"ROUTING REGRESSION: PUT ?partNumber&uploadId did not reach backend.UploadPart\\\")\\n231\\t\\t}\\n232\\t\\tif n := rb.objectPutCount(bucket, key); n != 0 {\\n233\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"ROUTING REGRESSION: backend.Put was called %d time(s) with the object key during a part upload — parts are being stored as whole objects (the ADR-002 corruption bug)\\\", n)\\n234\\t\\t}\\n235\\t}\\n236\\t\\n237\\t// TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification uploads three distinguishable parts\\n238\\t// (block-aligned intermediates + a non-aligned final part) through the real\\n239\\t// HTTP handler path (initiate → parts → complete), then verifies retrieval\\n240\\t// byte-for-byte via full GET, bounded Range GET across a part boundary,\\n241\\t// suffix Range GET (the parquet footer pattern), and HEAD length.\\n242\\t// Covers matrix rows U4, U9, R4, R7.\\n243\\tfunc TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification(t *testing.T) {\\n244\\t\\t_, rb, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n245\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"full-cycle.parquet\\\"\\n246\\t\\n247\\t\\t// Three parts with distinguishable patterns; final part non-aligned.\\n248\\t\\t// Scaled to 44MB+ total to match the actual failing scale from bf-1v6skf\\n249\\t\\t// (production litestream snapshot was 44,908,497 bytes and failed at block 256).\\n250\\t\\tconst targetTotal = 45 * 1024 * 1024 // 45MB target (slightly above production failure)\\n251\\t\\tconst block = 65536                  // encryption block size\\n252\\t\\tpartSize := 5 * 1024 * 1024          // 5MB per part (S3 minimum, matches production)\\n253\\t\\tfinalPartSize := targetTotal - (2 * partSize)\\n254\\t\\tsizes := []int{partSize, partSize, finalPartSize}\\n255\\t\\tvar plaintext []byte\\n256\\t\\tvar parts [][]byte\\n257\\t\\tfor p, size := range sizes {\\n258\\t\\t\\tpart := make([]byte, size)\\n259\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part {\\n260\\t\\t\\t\\tswitch p {\\n261\\t\\t\\t\\tcase 0:\\n262\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = byte(i & 0xFF)\\n263\\t\\t\\t\\tcase 1:\\n264\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = 0xFF - byte(i&0xFF)\\n265\\t\\t\\t\\tcase 2:\\n266\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif i%2 == 0 {\\n267\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = 0xAA\\n268\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n269\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = 0x55\\n270\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n271\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n272\\t\\t\\t}\\n273\\t\\t\\tparts = append(parts, part)\\n274\\t\\t\\tplaintext = append(plaintext, part...)\\n275\\t\\t}\\n276\\t\\n277\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n278\\t\\tvar etags []string\\n279\\t\\tfor i, part := range parts {\\n280\\t\\t\\tetags = append(etags, uploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, i+1, part))\\n281\\t\\t}\\n282\\t\\tcompleteMultipart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, etags)\\n283\\t\\n284\\t\\t// Full GET: byte-for-byte.\\n285\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/\\\"+bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key, nil)\\n286\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n287\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n288\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n289\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"GET after complete failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n290\\t\\t}\\n291\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\\n292\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"full GET content mismatch: got %d bytes, want %d; first divergence at %d\\\",\\n293\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Body.Len(), len(plaintext), firstDivergence(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext))\\n294\\t\\t}\\n295\\t\\n296\\t\\t// Bounded Range GET spanning the part-1/part-2 boundary.\\n297\\t\\tlo, hi := 2*block-500, 2*block+499\\n298\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/\\\"+bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key, nil)\\n299\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Range\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"bytes=%d-%d\\\", lo, hi))\\n300\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n301\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n302\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\\n303\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"range GET failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n304\\t\\t}\\n305\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext[lo:hi+1]) {\\n306\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"range GET across part boundary mismatch (bytes=%d-%d)\\\", lo, hi)\\n307\\t\\t}\\n308\\t\\n309\\t\\t// Suffix Range GET — the parquet footer access pattern (matrix R3/R4).\\n310\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/\\\"+bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key, nil)\\n311\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Range\\\", \\\"bytes=-1000\\\")\\n312\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n313\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n314\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\\n315\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"suffix range GET failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n316\\t\\t}\\n317\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext[len(plaintext)-1000:]) {\\n318\\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(\\\"suffix range GET (parquet footer pattern) content mismatch\\\")\\n319\\t\\t}\\n320\\t\\n321\\t\\t// HEAD: plaintext length, not ciphertext length.\\n322\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodHead, \\\"/\\\"+bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key, nil)\\n323\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n324\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n325\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n326\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"HEAD after complete failed: status %d\\\", w.Code)\\n327\\t\\t}\\n328\\t\\tif cl := w.Header().Get(\\\"Content-Length\\\"); cl != strconv.Itoa(len(plaintext)) {\\n329\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"HEAD Content-Length = %s, want %d\\\", cl, len(plaintext))\\n330\\t\\t}\\n331\\t\\n332\\t\\t_ = rb\\n333\\t}\\n334\\t\\n335\\t// TestMultipartSuspectPatterns documents and enforces rejection of the three\\n336\\t// multipart upload patterns that real SDKs use by default but ARMOR's\\n337\\t// per-part CTR-offset scheme does not support (docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md U6/U7/U8):\\n338\\t//\\n339\\t//\\tU6 out-of-order / parallel part upload  (boto3 uploads parts concurrently)\\n340\\t//\\tU7 part retry after network failure     (every SDK retries parts)\\n341\\t//\\tU8 non-block-aligned intermediate parts (any part size % 65536 != 0)\\n342\\t//\\n343\\t// UploadPart derives each part's CTR counter from arrival-order cumulative\\n344\\t// EncryptedBytes (handlers.go:2085), so all three would corrupt silently.\\n345\\t// The fix implemented in bf-59unr3 rejects these patterns with explicit 400\\n346\\t// errors rather than allowing silent corruption.\\n347\\tfunc TestMultipartSuspectPatterns(t *testing.T) {\\n348\\t\\tt.Run(\\\"U6_out_of_order_parts\\\", func(t *testing.T) {\\n349\\t\\t\\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n350\\t\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"out-of-order.dat\\\"\\n351\\t\\n352\\t\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n353\\t\\n354\\t\\t\\t// Upload part 1 first (succeeds)\\n355\\t\\t\\tpart1 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\\n356\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part1 {\\n357\\t\\t\\t\\tpart1[i] = 0xAA\\n358\\t\\t\\t}\\n359\\t\\t\\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part1)\\n360\\t\\n361\\t\\t\\t// Try to upload part 3 without uploading part 2 first (should fail)\\n362\\t\\t\\tpart3 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024)\\n363\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part3 {\\n364\\t\\t\\t\\tpart3[i] = 0xCC\\n365\\t\\t\\t}\\n366\\t\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?partNumber=3&uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, uploadID)\\n367\\t\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part3))\\n368\\t\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n369\\t\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n370\\t\\n371\\t\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\\n372\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected 400 BadRequest for out-of-order part, got %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n373\\t\\t\\t}\\n374\\t\\t\\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\\\"Parts must be uploaded in sequential order\\\")) {\\n375\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected error message about sequential order, got: %s\\\", w.Body.String())\\n376\\t\\t\\t}\\n377\\t\\t})\\n378\\t\\n379\\t\\tt.Run(\\\"U6_parallel_parts_simulation\\\", func(t *testing.T) {\\n380\\t\\t\\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n381\\t\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"parallel-sim.dat\\\"\\n382\\t\\n383\\t\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n384\\t\\n385\\t\\t\\t// Try to upload part 2 before part 1 (simulates parallel upload where part 2 arrives first)\\n386\\t\\t\\tpart2 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\\n387\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part2 {\\n388\\t\\t\\t\\tpart2[i] = 0xBB\\n389\\t\\t\\t}\\n390\\t\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?partNumber=2&uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, uploadID)\\n391\\t\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part2))\\n392\\t\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n393\\t\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n394\\t\\n395\\t\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\\n396\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected 400 BadRequest for part 2 before part 1, got %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n397\\t\\t\\t}\\n398\\t\\t\\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\\\"Expected part 1, got part 2\\\")) {\\n399\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected error message about expecting part 1, got: %s\\\", w.Body.String())\\n400\\t\\t\\t}\\n401\\t\\t})\\n402\\t\\n403\\t\\tt.Run(\\\"U7_part_retry\\\", func(t *testing.T) {\\n404\\t\\t\\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n405\\t\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"retry-test.dat\\\"\\n406\\t\\n407\\t\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n408\\t\\n409\\t\\t\\t// Upload part 1 successfully\\n410\\t\\t\\tpart1 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\\n411\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part1 {\\n412\\t\\t\\t\\tpart1[i] = 0xAA\\n413\\t\\t\\t}\\n414\\t\\t\\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part1)\\n415\\t\\n416\\t\\t\\t// Try to upload part 1 again (simulating retry after network failure)\\n417\\t\\t\\tpart1Retry := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024)\\n418\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part1Retry {\\n419\\t\\t\\t\\tpart1Retry[i] = 0xAA\\n420\\t\\t\\t}\\n421\\t\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?partNumber=1&uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, uploadID)\\n422\\t\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part1Retry))\\n423\\t\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n424\\t\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n425\\t\\n426\\t\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\\n427\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected 400 BadRequest for part retry, got %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n428\\t\\t\\t}\\n429\\t\\t\\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\\\"has already been uploaded\\\")) {\\n430\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected error message about already uploaded, got: %s\\\", w.Body.String())\\n431\\t\\t\\t}\\n432\\t\\t})\\n433\\t\\n434\\t\\tt.Run(\\\"U8_non_block_aligned_part\\\", func(t *testing.T) {\\n435\\t\\t\\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n436\\t\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"unaligned-test.dat\\\"\\n437\\t\\n438\\t\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n439\\t\\n440\\t\\t\\t// Try multipart upload thresholds and limits.\\n449\\tfunc TestB2Backend_MultipartThresholds(t *testing.T) {\\n450\\t\\t// S3 Multipart Upload Thresholds and Limits:\\n451\\t\\t//\\n452\\t\\t// Minimum object size for multipart upload:\\n453\\t\\t//   - 5MB (5,242,880 bytes) - recommended threshold\\n454\\t\\t//   - Objects smaller than this should use single-part PutObject\\n455\\t\\t//\\n456\\t\\t// Minimum part size (except last part):\\n457\\t\\t//   - 5MB (5,242,880 bytes)\\n458\\t\\t//   - All parts except the last must be at least this size\\n459\\t\\t//\\n460\\t\\t// Maximum part size:\\n461\\t\\t//   - 5GB (5,368,709,120 bytes)\\n462\\t\\t//\\n463\\t\\t// Maximum number of parts:\\n464\\t\\t//   - 10,000 parts per upload\\n465\\t\\t//\\n466\\t\\t// Maximum object size via multipart upload:\\n467\\t\\t//   - 5TB (5,497,558,138,880 bytes)\\n468\\t\\t//\\n469\\t\\t// Recommended part size for large files:\\n470\\t\\t//   - 10MB to 100MB is typical\\n471\\t\\t//   - Larger parts = fewer API calls = faster uploads\\n472\\t\\t//   - Smaller parts = better recovery from failures = more retryable\\n473\\t\\t//\\n474\\t\\t// For ARMOR:\\n475\\t\\t//   - Part sizes should be multiples of the encryption block size (4096 bytes)\\n476\\t\\t//   - This ensures proper alignment for encryption operations\\n477\\t\\t//   - Typical ARMOR part size: 5MB, 10MB, or 50MB (all multiples of 4096)\\n478\\t\\n479\\t\\tconst (\\n480\\t\\t\\tminMultipartObjectSize = 5 * 1024 * 1024     // 5MB\\n481\\t\\t\\tminPartSize           = 5 * 1024 * 1024     // 5MB\\n482\\t\\t\\tmaxPartSize           = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5GB\\n483\\t\\t\\tmaxPartsCount         = 10000\\n484\\t\\t\\tmaxObjectSize         = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5TB\\n485\\t\\t\\tarmorBlockSize        = 4096\\n486\\t\\t)\\n487\\t\\n488\\t\\tif minMultipartObjectSize < minPartSize {\\n489\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"Minimum multipart object size should be at least minimum part size\\\")\\n490\\t\\t}\\n491\\t\\n492\\t\\tif maxPartSize*maxPartsCount < maxObjectSize {\\n493\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"Maximum object size should be achievable with max parts and max part size\\\")\\n494\\t\\t}\\n495\\t\\n496\\t\\t// Verify common part sizes are multiples of ARMOR block size\\n497\\t\\tcommonPartSizes := []int64{\\n498\\t\\t\\t5 * 1024 * 1024,   // 5MB\\n499\\t\\t\\t10 * 1024 * 1024,  // 10MB\\n500\\t\\t\\t50 * 1024 * 1024,  // 50MB\\n501\\t\\t\\t100 * 1024 * 1024, // 100MB\\n502\\t\\t}\\n503\\t\\n504\\t\\tfor _, size := range commonPartSizes {\\n505\\t\\t\\tif size%int64(armorBlockSize) != 0 {\\n506\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Part size %d is not a multiple of ARMOR block size %d\\\", size, armorBlockSize)\\n507\\t\\t\\t}\\n508\\t\\t}\\n509\\t}\\n510\\t\\n511\\t// TestB2Backend_MultipartUploadWorkflow tests the complete multipart upload            cfg.MEK,\\n154\\t\\t\\tblockSize:         cfg.BlockSize,\\n155\\t\\t\\tinstanceID:        instanceID,\\n156\\t\\t\\tinterval:          cfg.Interval,\\n157\\t\\t\\tcanarySize:        cfg.CanarySize,\\n158\\t\\t\\tmaxRetries:        cfg.MaxRetries,\\n159\\t\\t\\tretryDelay:        cfg.RetryDelay,\\n160\\t\\t\\tmultipartInterval: cfg.MultipartInterval,\\n161\\t\\t\\tmultipartSize:     cfg.MultipartSize,\\n162\\t\\t\\tstopCh:            make(chan struct{}),\\n163\\t\\t\\tdoneCh:            make(chan struct{}),\\n164\\t\\t\\tstate: CanaryState{\\n165\\t\\t\\t\\tStatus:           StatusUnknown,\\n166\\t\\t\\t\\tMultipartHealthy: StatusUnknown,\\n167\\t\\t\\t\\tInstanceID:       instanceID,\\n168\\t\\t\\t},\\n169\\t\\t}\\n170\\t}\\n171\\t\\n172\\t// Start begins the periodic canary checks.\\n173\\t// It runs an initial check immediately, then periodically.\\n174\\tfunc (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) {\\n175\\t\\tgo func() {\\n176\\t\\t\\tdefer close(m.doneCh)\\n177\\t\\n178\\t\\t\\t// Initial checks\\n179\\t\\t\\tm.runCheck(ctx)\\n180\\t\\t\\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\\n181\\t\\n182\\t\\t\\tticker := time.NewTicker(m.interval)\\n183\\t\\t\\tmultipartTicker := time.NewTicker(m.multipartInterval)\\n184\\t\\t\\tdefer ticker.Stop()\\n185\\t\\t\\tdefer multipartTicker.Stop()\\n186\\t\\n187\\t\\t\\tfor {\\n188\\t\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n189\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-m.stopCh:\\n190\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n191\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-ctx.Done():\\n192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n193\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-ticker.C:\\n194\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tm.runCheck(ctx)\\n195\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-multipartTicker.C:\\n196\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\\n197\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n198\\t\\t\\t}\\n199\\t\\t}()\\n200\\t}\\n201\\t\\n202\\t// Stop stops the canary monitor.\\n203\\tfunc (m *Monitor) Stop() {\\n204\\t\\tclose(m.stopCh)\\n205\\t\\t<-m.doneCh\\n206\\t}\\n207\\t\\n208\\t// runCheck performs a single canary check with retries.\\n209\\tfunc (m *Monitor) runCheck(ctx context.Context) {\\n210\\t\\tvar lastErr error\\n211\\t\\n212\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryChecks()\\n213\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\\n214\\t\\n215\\t\\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\\n216\\t\\t\\tif attempt > 0 {\\n217\\t\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n218\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\\n219\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-ctx.Done():\\n220\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n221\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n222\\t\\t\\t}\\n223\\t\\n224\\t\\t\\tresult, err := m.check(ctx)\\n225\\t\\t\\tif err == nil {\\n226\\t\\t\\t\\tm.updateStateSuccess(result)\\n227\\t\\t\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(\\\"\\\")\\n228\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n229\\t\\t\\t}\\n230\\t\\t\\tlastErr = err\\n231\\t\\t}\\n232\\t\\n233\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryFailures()\\n234\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\\n235\\t\\tm.updateStateFailure(lastErr)\\n236\\t}\\n237\\t\\n238\\t// runMultipartCheck performs a single multipart canary check with retries.\\n239\\tfunc (m *Monitor) runMultipartCheck(ctx context.Context) {\\n240\\t\\tvar lastErr error\\n241\\t\\n242\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryChecks()\\n243\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\\n244\\t\\n245\\t\\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\\n246\\t\\t\\tif attempt > 0 {\\n247\\t\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n248\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\\n249\\t\\t\\t\\tcase <-ctx.Done():\\n250\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n251\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n252\\t\\t\\t}\\n253\\t\\n254\\t\\t\\tresult, err := m.checkMultipart(ctx)\\n255\\t\\t\\tif err == nil {\\n256\\t\\t\\t\\tm.updateMultipartStateSuccess(result)\\n257\\t\\t\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(\\\"\\\")\\n258\\t\\t\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(true)\\n259\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n260\\t\\t\\t}\\n261\\t\\t\\tlastErr = err\\n262\\t\\t}\\n263\\t\\n264\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryFailures()\\n265\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\\n266\\t\\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(false)\\n267\\t\\tm.updateMultipartStateFailure(lastErr)\\n268\\t}\\n269\\t\\n270\\t// check performs a single canary integrity check.\\n271\\tfunc (m *Monitor) check(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\\n272\\t\\tresult := &Result{\\n273\\t\\t\\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\\n274\\t\\t}\\n275\\t\\n276\\t\\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\\n277\\t\\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\\n278\\t\\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.canarySize)\\n279\\t\\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\\n280\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate canary content: %w\\\", err)\\n281\\t\\t}\\n282\\t\\n283\\t\\t// Embed timestamp for verification\\n284\\t\\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\\n285\\t\\n286\\t\\t// Generate unique key for this canary\\n287\\t\\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\\\".armor/canary/%s/%d\\\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\\n288\\t\\n289\\t\\t// Generate DEK and IV\\n290\\t\\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\\n291\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n292\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n293\\t\\t}\\n294\\t\\n295\\t\\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\\n296\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n297\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate IV: %w\\\", err)\\n298\\t\\t}\\n299\\t\\n300\\t\\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\\n301\\t\\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\\n302\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n303\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n304\\t\\t}\\n305\\t\\n306\\t\\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\\n307\\t\\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\\n308\\t\\n309\\t\\t// Create envelope header\\n310\\t\\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\\n311\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n312\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create header: %w\\\", err)\\n313\\t\\t}\\n314\\t\\n315\\t\\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\\n316\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n317\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode header: %w\\\", err)\\n318\\t\\t}\\n319\\t\\n320\\t\\t// Encrypt\\n321\\t\\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\\n322\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n323\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create encryptor: %w\\\", err)\\n324\\t\\t}\\n325\\t\\n326\\t\\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\\n327\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n328\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encrypt: %w\\\", err)\\n329\\t\\t}\\n330\\t\\n331\\t\\t// Build envelope\\n332\\t\\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\\n333\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\\n334\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\\n335\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\\n336\\t\\n337\\t\\t// Compute ETag\\n338\\t\\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\\n339\\t\\n340\\t\\t// Build metadata\\n341\\t\\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\\n342\\t\\t\\tVersion:       1,\\n343\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\\n344\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\\n345\\t\\t\\tContentType:   \\\"application/octet-stream\\\",\\n346\\t\\t\\tIV:            iv,\\n347\\t\\t\\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\\n348\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\\n349\\t\\t\\tETag:          etag,\\n350\\t\\t}).ToMetadata()\\n351\\t\\n352\\t\\t// Upload to B2 (direct, not through Cloudflare)\\n353\\t\\tuploadStart := time.Now()\\n354\\t\\tif err := m.backend.Put(ctx, m.bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\\n355\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to upload canary: %w\\\", err)\\n356\\t\\t}\\n357\\t\\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\\n358\\t\\n359\\t\\t// Download through Cloudflare (via GetRangeWithHeaders to capture CF-Cache-Status)\\n360\\t\\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\\n361\\t\\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\\n362\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n363\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to download canary: %w\\\", err)\\n364\\t\\t}\\n365\\t\\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\\n366\\t\\tbody.Close()\\n367\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n368\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to read downloaded canary: %w\\\", err)\\n369\\t\\t}\\n370\\t\\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\\n371\\t\\n372\\t\\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\\n373\\t\\t// CF-Cache-Status values: HIT, MISS, EXPIRED, STALE, BYPASS, REVALIDATED, UPDATING, IGNORED\\n374\\t\\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\\\"CF-Cache-Status\\\"]; ok {\\n375\\t\\t\\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \\\"HIT\\\" || cfStatus == \\\"STALE\\\" || cfStatus == \\\"REVALIDATED\\\")\\n376\\t\\t}\\n377\\t\\n378\\t\\t// Parse header\\n379\\t\\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\\n380\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n381\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to decode header: %w\\\", err)\\n382\\t\\t}\\n383\\t\\n384\\t\\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\\n385\\t\\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\\n386\\t\\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\\n387\\t\\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\\n388\\t\\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\\n389\\t\\n390\\t\\tif hmacEnd > int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\\n391\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\\\")\\n392\\t\\t}\\n393\\t\\n394\\t\\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\\n395\\t\\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\\n396\\t\\n397\\t\\t// Unwrap DEK\\n398\\t\\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\\n399\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n400\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n401\\t\\t}\\n402\\t\\n403\\t\\t// Create decryptor\\n404\\t\\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, m.blockSize)\\n405\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n406\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create decryptor: %w\\\", err)\\n407\\t\\t}\\n408\\t\\n409\\t\\t// Verify HMACs\\n410\\t\\tif err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\\n411\\t\\t\\tresult.HMACVerified = false\\n412\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"HMAC verification failed: %w\\\", err)\\n413\\t\\t}\\n414\\t\\tresult.HMACVerified = true\\n415\\t\\n416\\t\\t// Decrypt\\n417\\t\\tdecrypted, err := decryptor.Decrypt(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC)\\n418\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n419\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to decrypt: %w\\\", err)\\n420\\t\\t}\\n421\\t\\n422\\t\\t// Verify decrypted content matches original\\n423\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\\n424\\t\\t\\tresult.DecryptVerified = false\\n425\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"decrypted content does not match original\\\")\\n426\\t\\t}\\n427\\t\\tresult.DecryptVerified = true\\n428\\t\\n429\\t\\t// Verify plaintext SHA\\n430\\t\\tif err := downloadedHeader.VerifyPlaintextSHA(decrypted); err != nil {\\n431\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"plaintext SHA verification failed: %w\\\", err)\\n432\\t\\t}\\n433\\t\\n434\\t\\t// Clean up canary (best effort)\\n435\\t\\tgo func() {\\n436\\t\\t\\tcleanupCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)\\n437\\t\\t\\tdefer cancel()\\n438\\t\\t\\tm.backend.Delete(cleanupCtx, m.bucket, key)\\n439\\t\\t}()\\n440\\t\\n441\\t\\tresult.Status = StatusHealthy\\n442\\t\\n443\\t\\treturn result, nil\\n444\\t}\\n445\\t\\n446\\t// checkMultipart performs a multipart canary check to exercise the multipart upload path.\\n447\\tfunc (m *Monitor) checkMultipart(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\\n448\\t\\tresult := &Result{\\n449\\t\\t\\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\\n450\\t\\t}\\n451\\t\\n452\\t\\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\\n453\\t\\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\\n454\\t\\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.multipartSize)\\n455\\t\\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\\n456\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate multipart canary content: %w\\\", err)\\n457\\t\\t}\\n458\\t\\n459\\t\\t// Embed timestamp for verification\\n460\\t\\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\\n461\\t\\n462\\t\\t// Generate unique key for this canary\\n463\\t\\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\\\".armor/canary-multipart/%s/%d\\\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\\n464\\t\\n465\\t\\t// Generate DEK and IV\\n466\\t\\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\\n467\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n468\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n469\\t\\t}\\n470\\t\\n471\\t\\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\\n472\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n473\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to generate IV: %w\\\", err)\\n474\\t\\t}\\n475\\t\\n476\\t\\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\\n477\\t\\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\\n478\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n479\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n480\\t\\t}\\n481\\t\\n482\\t\\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\\n483\\t\\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\\n484\\t\\n485\\t\\t// Create envelope header\\n486\\t\\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\\n487\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n488\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create header: %w\\\", err)\\n489\\t\\t}\\n490\\t\\n491\\t\\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\\n492\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n493\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encode header: %w\\\", err)\\n494\\t\\t}\\n495\\t\\n496\\t\\t// Encrypt\\n497\\t\\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\\n498\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n499\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create encryptor: %w\\\", err)\\n500\\t\\t}\\n501\\t\\n502\\t\\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\\n503\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n504\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to encrypt: %w\\\", err)\\n505\\t\\t}\\n506\\t\\n507\\t\\t// Build envelope\\n508\\t\\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\\n509\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\\n510\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\\n511\\t\\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\\n512\\t\\n513\\t\\t// Compute ETag\\n514\\t\\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\\n515\\t\\n516\\t\\t// Build metadata\\n517\\t\\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\\n518\\t\\t\\tVersion:       1,\\n519\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\\n520\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\\n521\\t\\t\\tContentType:   \\\"application/octet-stream\\\",\\n522\\t\\t\\tIV:            iv,\\n523\\t\\t\\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\\n524\\t\\t\\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\\n525\\t\\t\\tETag:          etag,\\n526\\t\\t}).ToMetadata()\\n527\\t\\n528\\t\\t// Upload via multipart API (exercises create/upload/complete path)\\n529\\t\\tuploadStart := time.Now()\\n530\\t\\n531\\t\\t// Step 1: Create multipart upload\\n532\\t\\tuploadID, err := m.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, meta)\\n533\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n534\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create multipart upload: %w\\\", err)\\n535\\t\\t}\\n536\\t\\n537\\t\\t// Step 2: Upload parts (split into 2MB parts for reasonable part count)\\n538\\t\\tconst partSize = 2 * 1024 * 1024 // 2MB parts\\n539\\t\\tvar parts []backend.CompletedPart\\n540\\t\\tpartNum := int32(1)\\n541\\t\\n542\\t\\tfor offset := 0; offset < len(envelope); offset += partSize {\\n543\\t\\t\\tend := offset + partSize\\n544\\t\\t\\tif end > len(envelope) {\\n545\\t\\t\\t\\tend = len(envelope)\\n546\\t\\t\\t}\\n547\\t\\n548\\t\\t\\tpartData := envelope[offset:end]\\n549\\t\\t\\tpartETag, err := m.backend.UploadPart(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, partNum, bytes.NewReader(partData), int64(len(partData)))\\n550\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n551\\t\\t\\t\\t// Abort on failure\\n552\\t\\t\\t\\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\\n553\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to upload part %d: %w\\\", partNum, err)\\n554\\t\\t\\t}\\n555\\t\\n556\\t\\t\\tparts = append(parts, backend.CompletedPart{\\n557\\t\\t\\t\\tPartNumber: partNum,\\n558\\t\\t\\t\\tETag:       partETag,\\n559\\t\\t\\t})\\n560\\t\\n561\\t\\t\\tpartNum++\\n562\\t\\t}\\n563\\t\\n564\\t\\t// Step 3: Complete multipart upload\\n565\\t\\t_, err = m.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\\n566\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n567\\t\\t\\t// Abort on failure\\n568\\t\\t\\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\\n569\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to complete multipart upload: %w\\\", err)\\n570\\t\\t}\\n571\\t\\n572\\t\\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\\n573\\t\\n574\\t\\t// Download and verify (same verification as regular canary)\\n575\\t\\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\\n576\\t\\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\\n577\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n578\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to download multipart canary: %w\\\", err)\\n579\\t\\t}\\n580\\t\\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\\n581\\t\\tbody.Close()\\n582\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n583\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to read downloaded multipart canary: %w\\\", err)\\n584\\t\\t}\\n585\\t\\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\\n586\\t\\n587\\t\\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\\n588\\t\\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\\\"CF-Cache-Status\\\"]; ok {\\n589\\t\\t\\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \\\"HIT\\\" || cfStatus == \\\"STALE\\\" || cfStatus == \\\"REVALIDATED\\\")\\n590\\t\\t}\\n591\\t\\n592\\t\\t// Verify size matches\\n593\\t\\tif len(downloadedEnvelope) != len(envelope) {\\n594\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"multipart download size mismatch: got %d, expected %d\\\", len(downloadedEnvelope), len(envelope))\\n595\\t\\t}\\n596\\t\\n597\\t\\t// Byte-for-byte verification (critical for multipart integrity)\\n598\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(downloadedEnvelope, envelope) {\\n599\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"multipart content byte-for-byte verification failed\\\")\\n600\\t\\t}\\n601\\t\\n602\\t\\t// Parse header\\n603\\t\\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\\n604\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n605\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to decode header: %w\\\", err)\\n606\\t\\t}\\n607\\t\\n608\\t\\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\\n609\\t\\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\\n610\\t\\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\\n611\\t\\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\\n612\\t\\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\\n613\\t\\n614\\t\\tif hmacEnd > int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\\n615\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\\\")\\n616\\t\\t}\\n617\\t\\n618\\t\\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\\n619\\t\\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\\n620\\t\\n621\\t\\t// Unwrap DEK\\n622\\t\\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\\n623\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n624\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\\\", err)\\n625\\t\\t}\\n626\\t\\n627\\t\\t// Create decryptor\\n628\\t\\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, m.blockSize)\\n629\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n630\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create decryptor: %w\\\", err)\\n631\\t\\t}\\n632\\t\\n633\\t\\t// Verify HMACs\\n634\\t\\tif err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\\n635\\t\\t\\tresult.HMACVerified = false\\n636\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"HMAC verification failed: %w\\\", err)\\n637\\t\\t}\\n638\\t\\tresult.HMACVerified = true\\n639\\t\\n640\\t\\t// Decrypt\\n641\\t\\tdecrypted, err := decryptor.Decrypt(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC)\\n642\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n643\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to decrypt: %w\\\", err)\\n644\\t\\t}\\n645\\t\\n646\\t\\t// Verify decrypted content matches original\\n647\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\\n648\\t\\t\\tresult.DecryptVerified = Parts must be at least 5MB\\n16\\t//     (except the last part) and part numbers must be >= 1.\\n17\\t//\\n18\\t//   CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts) -> finalETag\\n19\\t//     Completes a multipart upload by combining all uploaded parts. Parts must\\n20\\t//     be sorted by part number and include all uploaded parts with their ETags.\\n21\\t//\\n22\\t//   AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\\n23\\t//     Cancels a multipart upload and releases all storage associated with it.\\n24\\t//     Should be called for cleanup when an upload fails.\\n25\\t//\\n26\\t// Helper Functions\\n27\\t//\\n28\\t// The MultipartUploadHelper provides higher-level helper functions for common\\n29\\t// multipart upload scenarios:\\n30\\t//\\n31\\t//   NewMultipartUploadHelper(backend, bucket) -> helper\\n32\\t//     Creates a helper for a specific bucket.\\n33\\t//\\n34\\t//   helper.CreateUpload(ctx, key, metadata) -> uploadInfo\\n35\\t//     Creates a new multipart upload.\\n36\\t//\\n37\\t//   helper.UploadPart(ctx, uploadInfo, partNumber, data) -> error\\n38\\t//     Uploads a single part (includes validation).\\n39\\t//\\n40\\t//   helper.CompleteUpload(ctx, uploadInfo) -> finalETag\\n41\\t//     Completes the multipart upload.\\n42\\t//\\n43\\t//   helper.AbortUpload(ctx, uploadInfo) -> error\\n44\\t//     Cancels the multipart upload.\\n45\\t//\\n46\\t//   helper.CanaryUpload(ctx, key, data) -> error\\n47\\t//     Performs a simple canary test of the multipart upload system.\\n48\\t//\\n49\\t//   VerifyMultipartUpload(ctx, backend, bucket, key, data, partSize) -> error\\n50\\t//     Full verification: uploads via multipart, downloads back, and verifies integrity.\\n51\\t//\\n52\\t// S3 Multipart Upload Thresholds and Limits\\n53\\t//\\n54\\t//   Minimum part size (except last part): 5MB (5,242,880 bytes)\\n55\\t//   Maximum part size: 5GB (5,368,709,120 bytes)\\n56\\t//   Maximum number of parts: 10,000\\n57\\t//   Maximum object size via multipart: 5TB (5,497,558,138,880 bytes)\\n58\\t//   Recommended part size: 10MB to 100MB for efficiency\\n59\\t//\\n60\\t// For ARMOR:\\n61\\t//   Part sizes should be multiples of the encryption block size (4096 bytes)\\n62\\t//   Typical ARMOR part sizes: 5MB, 10MB, 50MB (all multiples of 4096)\\n63\\t//\\n64\\t// Usage Examples\\n65\\t//\\n66\\t// Basic multipart upload:\\n67\\t//\\n68\\t//   helper := NewMultipartUploadHelper(backend, \\\"my-bucket\\\")\\n69\\t//\\n70\\t//   // Create upload\\n71\\t//   info, err := helper.CreateUpload(ctx, \\\"test-key\\\", map[string]string{\\n72\\t//       \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/octet-stream\\\",\\n73\\t//   })\\n74\\t//   if err != nil {\\n75\\t//       return err\\n76\\t//   }\\n77\\t//\\n78\\t//   // Upload parts\\n79\\t// err := helper.CompleteUpload(ctx, info)\\n90\\t//   if err != nil {\\n91\\t//       return err\\n92\\t//   }\\n93\\t//\\n94\\t// Canary monitor usage:\\n95\\t//\\n96\\t//   // Simple canary }\\n105\\t//\\n106\\t//   // Full verification test\\n107\\t//   err = VerifyMultipartUpload(ctx, backend, \\\"my-bucket\\\", testKey, testData, 5*1024*1024)\\n108\\t//   if err != nil {\\n109\\t// - 10\\n452\\t\\t\\tif contextStart < 0 {\\n453\\t\\t\\t\\tcontextStart = 0\\n454\\t\\t\\t}\\n455\\t\\t\\tcontextEnd := mismatchOffset + 10\\n456\\t\\t\\tif contextEnd > len(downloadedContent)
