nil\\n83\\t}\\n84\\t\\n85\\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, completed []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\\n86\\t\\tr.rmu.Lock()\\n87\\t\\tparts, ok := r.uploads[uploadID]\\n88\\t\\tif !ok {\\n89\\t\\t\\tr.rmu.Unlock()\\n90\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"no such upload: %s\\\", uploadID)\\n91\\t\\t}\\n92\\t\\tvar assembled []byte\\n93\\t\\tfor _, p := range completed {\\n94\\t\\t\\tdata, ok := parts[p.PartNumber]\\n95\\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n96\\t\\t\\t\\tr.rmu.Unlock()\\n97\\t\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"missing part %d\\\", p.PartNumber)\\n98\\t\\t\\t}\\n99\\t\\t\\tassembled = append(assembled, data...)\\n100\\t\\t}\\n101\\t\\tdelete(r.uploads, uploadID)\\n102\\t\\tr.rmu.Unlock()\\n103\\t\\n104\\t\\t// Store assembled object exactly as B2 would, preserving no metadata —\\n105\\t\\t// the handler applies ARMOR metadata afterwards via Copy.\\n106\\t\\tr.mu.Lock()\\n107\\t\\tr.objects[bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key] = assembled\\n108\\t\\tif _, ok := r.meta[bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key]; !ok {\\n109\\t\\t\\tr.meta[bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key] = map[string]string{}\\n110\\t\\t}\\n111\\t\\tr.mu.Unlock()\\n112\\t\\treturn \\\"etag-assembled\\\", nil\\n113\\t}\\n114\\t\\n115\\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\\n116\\t\\tr.rmu.Lock()\\n117\\t\\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\\n118\\t\\tdelete(r.uploads, uploadID)\\n119\\t\\treturn nil\\n120\\t}\\n121\\t\\n122\\t// objectPutCount returns how many times Put was called with exactly this key.\\n123\\tfunc (r *recordingBackend) objectPutCount(bucket, key string) int {\\n124\\t\\tr.rmu.Lock()\\n125\\t\\tdefer r.rmu.Unlock()\\n126\\t\\tn := 0\\n127\\t\\tfor _, k := range r.putKeys {\\n128\\t\\t\\tif k == bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key {\\n129\\t\\t\\t\\tn++\\n130\\t\\t\\t}\\n131\\t\\t}\\n132\\t\\treturn n\\n133\\t}\\n134\\t\\n135\\tfunc recordingTestSetup(t *testing.T) (*config.Config, *recordingBackend, *handlers.Handlers) {\\n136\\t\\tt.Helper()\\n137\\t\\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\\n138\\t\\tif _, err := rand.Read(mek); err != nil {\\n139\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to generate MEK: %v\\\", err)\\n140\\t\\t}\\n141\\t\\tcfg := &config.Config{\\n142\\t\\t\\tBlockSize:     65536,\\n143\\t\\t\\tAuthAccessKey: \\\"test-access-key\\\",\\n144\\t\\t\\tAuthSecretKey: \\\"test-secret-key\\\",\\n145\\t\\t}\\n146\\t\\trb := newRecordingBackend()\\n147\\t\\tcache := backend.NewMetadataCache(1000, 300)\\n148\\t\\tfooterCache := backend.NewFooterCache(1000, 300)\\n149\\t\\tkm, err := keymanager.New(mek, nil, nil)\\n150\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n151\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to create key manager: %v\\\", err)\\n152\\t\\t}\\n153\\t\\th := handlers.New(cfg, rb, cache, footerCache, km, nil)\\n154\\t\\treturn cfg, rb, h\\n155\\t}\\n156\\t\\n157\\tfunc initiateMultipart(t *testing.T, h *handlers.Handlers, bucket, key string) string {\\n158\\t\\tt.Helper()\\n159\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?uploads\\\", bucket, key), nil)\\n160\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/octet-stream\\\")\\n161\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n162\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n163\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n164\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"CreateMultipartUpload failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n165\\t\\t}\\n166\\t\\tvar result struct {\\n167\\t\\t\\tUploadID string `xml:\\\"UploadId\\\"`\\n168\\t\\t}\\n169\\t\\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &result); err != nil {\\n170\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to parse initiate response: %v\\\", err)\\n171\\t\\t}\\n172\\t\\tif result.UploadID == \\\"\\\" {\\n173\\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(\\\"empty UploadId in initiate response\\\")\\n174\\t\\t}\\n175\\t\\treturn result.UploadID\\n176\\t}\\n177\\t\\n178\\tfunc uploadPart(t *testing.T, h *handlers.Handlers, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int, body []byte) string {\\n179\\t\\tt.Helper()\\n180\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?partNumber=%d&uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, partNumber, uploadID)\\n181\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(body))\\n182\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n183\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n184\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n185\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"UploadPart %d failed: status %d: %s\\\", partNumber, w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n186\\t\\t}\\n187\\t\\tetag := w.Header().Get(\\\"ETag\\\")\\n188\\t\\tif etag == \\\"\\\" {\\n189\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"UploadPart %d returned no ETag\\\", partNumber)\\n190\\t\\t}\\n191\\t\\treturn etag\\n192\\t}\\n193\\t\\n194\\tfunc completeMultipart(t *testing.T, h *handlers.Handlers, bucket, key, uploadID string, etags []string) {\\n195\\t\\tt.Helper()\\n196\\t\\tvar xmlBody bytes.Buffer\\n197\\t\\txmlBody.WriteString(\\\"<CompleteMultipartUpload>\\\")\\n198\\t\\tfor i, etag := range etags {\\n199\\t\\t\\tfmt.Fprintf(&xmlBody, \\\"<Part><PartNumber>%d</PartNumber><ETag>%s</ETag></Part>\\\", i+1, etag)\\n200\\t\\t}\\n201\\t\\txmlBody.WriteString(\\\"</CompleteMultipartUpload>\\\")\\n202\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, uploadID)\\n203\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, url, &xmlBody)\\n204\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n205\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n206\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n207\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"CompleteMultipartUpload failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n208\\t\\t}\\n209\\t}\\n210\\t\\n211\\t// TestUploadPartRoutingNeverFallsThroughToPut is the ADR-002 regression\\n212\\t// tripwire. The 2026-06 bug routed `PUT ?partNumber&uploadId` to PutObject;\\n213\\t// this asserts the part request reaches UploadPart and that Put is never\\n214\\t// invoked for the object key itself (state/sidecar writes use other keys).\\n215\\tfunc TestUploadPartRoutingNeverFallsThroughToPut(t *testing.T) {\\n216\\t\\t_, rb, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n217\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"routing-tripwire.dat\\\"\\n218\\t\\n219\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n220\\t\\tpart := make([]byte, 2*65536) // two full blocks\\n221\\t\\tfor i := range part {\\n222\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = byte(i % 251)\\n223\\t\\t}\\n224\\t\\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part)\\n225\\t\\n226\\t\\trb.rmu.Lock()\\n227\\t\\tpartCalls := len(rb.partKeys)\\n228\\t\\trb.rmu.Unlock()\\n229\\t\\tif partCalls == 0 {\\n230\\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(\\\"ROUTING REGRESSION: PUT ?partNumber&uploadId did not reach backend.UploadPart\\\")\\n231\\t\\t}\\n232\\t\\tif n := rb.objectPutCount(bucket, key); n != 0 {\\n233\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"ROUTING REGRESSION: backend.Put was called %d time(s) with the object key during a part upload — parts are being stored as whole objects (the ADR-002 corruption bug)\\\", n)\\n234\\t\\t}\\n235\\t}\\n236\\t\\n237\\t// TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification uploads three distinguishable parts\\n238\\t// (block-aligned intermediates + a non-aligned final part) through the real\\n239\\t// HTTP handler path (initiate → parts → complete), then verifies retrieval\\n240\\t// byte-for-byte via full GET, bounded Range GET across a part boundary,\\n241\\t// suffix Range GET (the parquet footer pattern), and HEAD length.\\n242\\t// Covers matrix rows U4, U9, R4, R7.\\n243\\tfunc TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification(t *testing.T) {\\n244\\t\\t_, rb, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n245\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"full-cycle.parquet\\\"\\n246\\t\\n247\\t\\t// Three parts with distinguishable patterns; final part non-aligned.\\n248\\t\\t// Scaled to 44MB+ total to match the actual failing scale from bf-1v6skf\\n249\\t\\t// (production litestream snapshot was 44,908,497 bytes and failed at block 256).\\n250\\t\\tconst targetTotal = 45 * 1024 * 1024 // 45MB target (slightly above production failure)\\n251\\t\\tconst block = 65536                  // encryption block size\\n252\\t\\tpartSize := 5 * 1024 * 1024          // 5MB per part (S3 minimum, matches production)\\n253\\t\\tfinalPartSize := targetTotal - (2 * partSize)\\n254\\t\\tsizes := []int{partSize, partSize, finalPartSize}\\n255\\t\\tvar plaintext []byte\\n256\\t\\tvar parts [][]byte\\n257\\t\\tfor p, size := range sizes {\\n258\\t\\t\\tpart := make([]byte, size)\\n259\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part {\\n260\\t\\t\\t\\tswitch p {\\n261\\t\\t\\t\\tcase 0:\\n262\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = byte(i & 0xFF)\\n263\\t\\t\\t\\tcase 1:\\n264\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = 0xFF - byte(i&0xFF)\\n265\\t\\t\\t\\tcase 2:\\n266\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif i%2 == 0 {\\n267\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = 0xAA\\n268\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n269\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = 0x55\\n270\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n271\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n272\\t\\t\\t}\\n273\\t\\t\\tparts = append(parts, part)\\n274\\t\\t\\tplaintext = append(plaintext, part...)\\n275\\t\\t}\\n276\\t\\n277\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n278\\t\\tvar etags []string\\n279\\t\\tfor i, part := range parts {\\n280\\t\\t\\tetags = append(etags, uploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, i+1, part))\\n281\\t\\t}\\n282\\t\\tcompleteMultipart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, etags)\\n283\\t\\n284\\t\\t// Full GET: byte-for-byte.\\n285\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/\\\"+bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key, nil)\\n286\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n287\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n288\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n289\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"GET after complete failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n290\\t\\t}\\n291\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\\n292\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"full GET content mismatch: got %d bytes, want %d; first divergence at %d\\\",\\n293\\t\\t\\t\\tw.Body.Len(), len(plaintext), firstDivergence(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext))\\n294\\t\\t}\\n295\\t\\n296\\t\\t// Bounded Range GET spanning the part-1/part-2 boundary.\\n297\\t\\tlo, hi := 2*block-500, 2*block+499\\n298\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/\\\"+bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key, nil)\\n299\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Range\\\", fmt.Sprintf(\\\"bytes=%d-%d\\\", lo, hi))\\n300\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n301\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n302\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\\n303\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"range GET failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n304\\t\\t}\\n305\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext[lo:hi+1]) {\\n306\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"range GET across part boundary mismatch (bytes=%d-%d)\\\", lo, hi)\\n307\\t\\t}\\n308\\t\\n309\\t\\t// Suffix Range GET — the parquet footer access pattern (matrix R3/R4).\\n310\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/\\\"+bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key, nil)\\n311\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Range\\\", \\\"bytes=-1000\\\")\\n312\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n313\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n314\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\\n315\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"suffix range GET failed: status %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n316\\t\\t}\\n317\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext[len(plaintext)-1000:]) {\\n318\\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(\\\"suffix range GET (parquet footer pattern) content mismatch\\\")\\n319\\t\\t}\\n320\\t\\n321\\t\\t// HEAD: plaintext length, not ciphertext length.\\n322\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodHead, \\\"/\\\"+bucket+\\\"/\\\"+key, nil)\\n323\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n324\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n325\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n326\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"HEAD after complete failed: status %d\\\", w.Code)\\n327\\t\\t}\\n328\\t\\tif cl := w.Header().Get(\\\"Content-Length\\\"); cl != strconv.Itoa(len(plaintext)) {\\n329\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"HEAD Content-Length = %s, want %d\\\", cl, len(plaintext))\\n330\\t\\t}\\n331\\t\\n332\\t\\t_ = rb\\n333\\t}\\n334\\t\\n335\\t// TestMultipartSuspectPatterns documents and enforces rejection of the three\\n336\\t// multipart upload patterns that real SDKs use by default but ARMOR's\\n337\\t// per-part CTR-offset scheme does not support (docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md U6/U7/U8):\\n338\\t//\\n339\\t//\\tU6 out-of-order / parallel part upload  (boto3 uploads parts concurrently)\\n340\\t//\\tU7 part retry after network failure     (every SDK retries parts)\\n341\\t//\\tU8 non-block-aligned intermediate parts (any part size % 65536 != 0)\\n342\\t//\\n343\\t// UploadPart derives each part's CTR counter from arrival-order cumulative\\n344\\t// EncryptedBytes (handlers.go:2085), so all three would corrupt silently.\\n345\\t// The fix implemented in bf-59unr3 rejects these patterns with explicit 400\\n346\\t// errors rather than allowing silent corruption.\\n347\\tfunc TestMultipartSuspectPatterns(t *testing.T) {\\n348\\t\\tt.Run(\\\"U6_out_of_order_parts\\\", func(t *testing.T) {\\n349\\t\\t\\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n350\\t\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"out-of-order.dat\\\"\\n351\\t\\n352\\t\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n353\\t\\n354\\t\\t\\t// Upload part 1 first (succeeds)\\n355\\t\\t\\tpart1 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\\n356\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part1 {\\n357\\t\\t\\t\\tpart1[i] = 0xAA\\n358\\t\\t\\t}\\n359\\t\\t\\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part1)\\n360\\t\\n361\\t\\t\\t// Try to upload part 3 without uploading part 2 first (should fail)\\n362\\t\\t\\tpart3 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024)\\n363\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part3 {\\n364\\t\\t\\t\\tpart3[i] = 0xCC\\n365\\t\\t\\t}\\n366\\t\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?partNumber=3&uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, uploadID)\\n367\\t\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part3))\\n368\\t\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n369\\t\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n370\\t\\n371\\t\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\\n372\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected 400 BadRequest for out-of-order part, got %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n373\\t\\t\\t}\\n374\\t\\t\\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\\\"Parts must be uploaded in sequential order\\\")) {\\n375\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected error message about sequential order, got: %s\\\", w.Body.String())\\n376\\t\\t\\t}\\n377\\t\\t})\\n378\\t\\n379\\t\\tt.Run(\\\"U6_parallel_parts_simulation\\\", func(t *testing.T) {\\n380\\t\\t\\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n381\\t\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"parallel-sim.dat\\\"\\n382\\t\\n383\\t\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n384\\t\\n385\\t\\t\\t// Try to upload part 2 before part 1 (simulates parallel upload where part 2 arrives first)\\n386\\t\\t\\tpart2 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\\n387\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part2 {\\n388\\t\\t\\t\\tpart2[i] = 0xBB\\n389\\t\\t\\t}\\n390\\t\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?partNumber=2&uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, uploadID)\\n391\\t\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part2))\\n392\\t\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n393\\t\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n394\\t\\n395\\t\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\\n396\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected 400 BadRequest for part 2 before part 1, got %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n397\\t\\t\\t}\\n398\\t\\t\\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\\\"Expected part 1, got part 2\\\")) {\\n399\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected error message about expecting part 1, got: %s\\\", w.Body.String())\\n400\\t\\t\\t}\\n401\\t\\t})\\n402\\t\\n403\\t\\tt.Run(\\\"U7_part_retry\\\", func(t *testing.T) {\\n404\\t\\t\\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n405\\t\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"retry-test.dat\\\"\\n406\\t\\n407\\t\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n408\\t\\n409\\t\\t\\t// Upload part 1 successfully\\n410\\t\\t\\tpart1 := make([]byte, 5*1024*1024) // 5MiB, block-aligned\\n411\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part1 {\\n412\\t\\t\\t\\tpart1[i] = 0xAA\\n413\\t\\t\\t}\\n414\\t\\t\\tuploadPart(t, h, bucket, key, uploadID, 1, part1)\\n415\\t\\n416\\t\\t\\t// Try to upload part 1 again (simulating retry after network httptest.NewRecorder()\\n424\\t\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n425\\t\\n426\\t\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\\n427\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected 400 BadRequest for part retry, got %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n428\\t\\t\\t}\\n429\\t\\t\\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\\\"has already been uploaded\\\")) {\\n430\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected error message about already uploaded, got: %s\\\", w.Body.String())\\n431\\t\\t\\t}\\n432\\t\\t})\\n433\\t\\n434\\t\\tt.Run(\\\"U8_non_block_aligned_part\\\", func(t *testing.T) {\\n435\\t\\t\\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n436\\t\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"unaligned-test.dat\\\"\\n437\\t\\n438\\t\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n439\\t\\n440\\t\\t\\t// Try to upload a part with non-block-aligned size (10,000,000 bytes)\\n441\\t\\t\\t// 10,000,000 % 65536 = 16976 (not aligned)\\n442\\t\\t\\tpart := make([]byte, 10_000_000)\\n443\\t\\t\\tfor i := range part {\\n444\\t\\t\\t\\tpart[i] = 0xAA\\n445\\t\\t\\t}\\n446\\t\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?partNumber=1&uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, uploadID)\\n447\\t\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part))\\n448\\t\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n449\\t\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n450\\t\\n451\\t\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {\\n452\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected 400 BadRequest for non-block-aligned part, got %d: %s\\\", w.Code, w.Body.String())\\n453\\t\\t\\t}\\n454\\t\\t\\tif !bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\\\"not a multiple of the block size\\\")) {\\n455\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Expected error message about block alignment, got: %s\\\", w.Body.String())\\n456\\t\\t\\t}\\n457\\t\\t})\\n458\\t\\n459\\t\\tt.Run(\\\"U8_zero_byte_part_allowed\\\", func(t *testing.T) {\\n460\\t\\t\\t// Edge case: zero-byte parts should be allowed (can happen with empty files)\\n461\\t\\t\\t_, _, h := recordingTestSetup(t)\\n462\\t\\t\\tbucket, key := \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"zero-byte.dat\\\"\\n463\\t\\n464\\t\\t\\tuploadID := initiateMultipart(t, h, bucket, key)\\n465\\t\\n466\\t\\t\\t// Zero-byte part should not trigger the alignment check\\n467\\t\\t\\tpart := make([]byte, 0)\\n468\\t\\t\\turl := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"/%s/%s?partNumber=1&uploadId=%s\\\", bucket, key, uploadID)\\n469\\t\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, url, bytes.NewReader(part))\\n470\\t\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n471\\t\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n472\\t\\n473\\t\\t\\t// Zero-byte parts should fail for a different reason (not alignment)\\n474\\t\\t\\t// The actual error might be about empty body or size, but not the alignment check\\n475\\t\\t\\tif w.Code == http.StatusBadRequest && bytes.Contains(w.Body.Bytes(), []byte(\\\"not a multiple of the block size\\\")) {\\n476\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Zero-byte part should not trigger block alignment error: %s\\\", w.Body.String())\\n477\\t\\t\\t}\\n478\\t\\t})\\n479\\t}\\n480\\t\\n481\\tfunc firstDivergence(a, b []byte) int {\\n482\\t\\tn := len(a)\\n483\\t\\tif len(b) < n {\\n484\\t\\t\\tn = len(b)\\n485\\t\\t}\\n486\\t\\tfor i := 0; i < n; i++ {\\n487\\t\\t\\tif a[i] != b[i] {\\n488\\t\\t\\t\\treturn i\\n489\\t\\t\\t}\\n490\\t\\t}\\n491\\t\\treturn n\\n492\\t}\\n493\\t\\n494\\t// TestMultipartPartBoundaryDebug tests decryption at the exact part boundary.\\n495\\tfunc multipart upload thresholds and limits.\\n449\\tfunc TestB2Backend_MultipartThresholds(t *testing.T) {\\n450\\t\\t// S3 Multipart Upload Thresholds and Limits:\\n451\\t\\t//\\n452\\t\\t// Minimum object size for multipart upload:\\n453\\t\\t//   - 5MB (5,242,880 bytes) - recommended threshold\\n454\\t\\t//   - Objects smaller than this should use single-part PutObject\\n455\\t\\t//\\n456\\t\\t// Minimum part size (except last part):\\n457\\t\\t//   - 5MB (5,242,880 bytes)\\n458\\t\\t//   - All parts except the last must be at least this size\\n459\\t\\t//\\n460\\t\\t// Maximum part size:\\n461\\t\\t//   - 5GB (5,368,709,120 bytes)\\n462\\t\\t//\\n463\\t\\t// Maximum number of parts:\\n464\\t\\t//   - 10,000 parts per upload\\n465\\t\\t//\\n466\\t\\t// Maximum object size via multipart upload:\\n467\\t\\t//   - 5TB (5,497,558,138,880 bytes)\\n468\\t\\t//\\n469\\t\\t// Recommended part size for large files:\\n470\\t\\t//   - 10MB to 100MB is typical\\n471\\t\\t//   - Larger parts = fewer API calls = faster uploads\\n472\\t\\t//   - Smaller parts = better recovery from failures = more retryable\\n473\\t\\t//\\n474\\t\\t// For ARMOR:\\n475\\t\\t//   - Part sizes should be multiples of the encryption block size (4096 bytes)\\n476\\t\\t//   - This ensures proper alignment for encryption operations\\n477\\t\\t//   - Typical ARMOR part size: 5MB, 10MB, or 50MB (all multiples of 4096)\\n478\\t\\n479\\t\\tconst (\\n480\\t\\t\\tminMultipartObjectSize = 5 * 1024 * 1024     // 5MB\\n481\\t\\t\\tminPartSize           = 5 * 1024 * 1024     // 5MB\\n482\\t\\t\\tmaxPartSize           = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5GB\\n483\\t\\t\\tmaxPartsCount         = 10000\\n484\\t\\t\\tmaxObjectSize         = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5TB\\n485\\t\\t\\tarmorBlockSize        = 4096\\n486\\t\\t)\\n487\\t\\n488\\t\\tif minMultipartObjectSize < minPartSize {\\n489\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"Minimum multipart object size should be at least minimum part size\\\")\\n490\\t\\t}\\n491\\t\\n492\\t\\tif maxPartSize*maxPartsCount < maxObjectSize {\\n493\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"Maximum object size should be achievable with max parts and max part size\\\")\\n494\\t\\t}\\n495\\t\\n496\\t\\t// Verify common part sizes are multiples of ARMOR block size\\n497\\t\\tcommonPartSizes := []int64{\\n498\\t\\t\\t5 * 1024 * 1024,   // 5MB\\n499\\t\\t\\t10 * 1024 * 1024,  // 10MB\\n500\\t\\t\\t50 * 1024 * 1024,  // 50MB\\n501\\t\\t\\t100 * 1024 * 1024, // 100MB\\n502\\t\\t}\\n503\\t\\n504\\t\\tfor _, size := range commonPartSizes {\\n505\\t\\t\\tif size%int64(armorBlockSize) != 0 {\\n506\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"Part size %d is not a multiple of ARMOR block size %d\\\", size, armorBlockSize)\\n507\\t\\t\\t}\\n508\\t\\t}\\n509\\t}\\n510\\t\\n511\\t// TestB2Backend_MultipartUploadWorkflow tests the complete multipart upload Here's what I discovered:\n\n## 1. Current Monitor/Canary Upload Code\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/canary/canary.go`\n\nThe canary monitor has **two types of checks**:\n\n### Regular Canary Check (`check()` method, lines 271-444)\n- Generates random canary content (default 1024 bytes)\n- Encrypts with ARMOR envelope encryption\n- Uploads directly via `backend.Put()`\n- Downloads through Cloudflare (via `backend.GetRangeWithHeaders()`)\n- Verifies decryption integrity\n- Cleans up asynchronously\n\n### Multipart Canary Check (`checkMultipart()` method, lines 446-669)\n- Generates larger canary content (default 6MB - above 5MB threshold)\n- Uses **multipart upload API** with 2MB parts:\n  - `CreateMultipartUpload()` to initiate\n  - `UploadPart()` for each part (in series)\n  - `CompleteMultipartUpload()` to finish\n- Downloads and verifies byte-for-byte integrity\n- Tracks separate `MultipartHealthy` status\n\n**Monitor Configuration** (lines 104-116):\n```go\ntype Config struct {\n    Interval          time.Duration // Regular check (default 5 minutes)\n    MultipartInterval time.Duration // Multipart check (default 1 hour)\n    MultipartSize     int           // Multipart canary size (default 6MB)\n    // ... other fields\n}\n```\n\n## 2. Multipart Upload Helper Functions\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/backend/multipart_helpers.go`\n\n### Core Helper Functions:\n- **`MultipartUploadHelper`** struct - High-level wrapper for multipart operations\n- **`CreateUpload()`** - Initiates multipart upload\n- **`UploadPart()`** - Uploads single part with 5MB minimum validation\n- **`CompleteUpload()`** - Finishes multipart upload\n- **`AbortUpload()`** - Cleanup on failure\n\n### Specialized Functions:\n- **`CanaryUpload()`** (lines 139-182) - Quick canary test for multipart system\n- **`VerifyMultipartUpload()`** (lines 186-250) - Full integrity verification: uploads data via multipart, downloads back, verifies byte-for-byte match\n- **`UploadPartsInSeries()`** (lines 103-135) - Uploads all parts sequentially\n\n### Constants & Utilities:\n- **`GetMultipartUploadConstants()`** - Returns S3 limits (5GB max part size, 10K max parts, etc.)\n- **`CalculateOptimalPartSize()`** - Determines optimal part size for file size\n\n## 3. Backend Interface & Low-Level Primitives\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/backend/backend.go` (lines 87-103)\n\nThe Backend interface defines the core multipart operations:\n\n```go\nCreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, meta) -> uploadID\nUploadPart(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, partNumber, body, size) -> etag\nCompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts) -> finalETag\nAbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID) -> error\nListParts(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID) -> partsList\nListMultipartUploads(ctx, bucket) -> uploadsList\n```\n\n**CompletedPart struct** (lines 146-149):\n```go\ntype CompletedPart struct {\n    PartNumber int32\n    ETag       string\n}\n```\n\n## 4. Testing Patterns & Integration Tests\n\n### Backend Tests (`/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/backend/b2_multipart_test.go`):\n- **Mock S3 client** with configurable behavior\n- **Error case testing** (NoSuchUpload, EntityTooSmall, InvalidPart, etc.)\n- **Context cancellation** tests\n- **Complete workflow tests** (create → upload parts → complete)\n- **Mock backend** (`NewMockB2BackendForTesting()`) for testing without credentials\n\n### Integration Tests (`/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/backend/multipart_integration_test.go`):\n- **`TestMultipartUpload`** - 15MB 3-part test with distinguishable patterns per part\n- **`TestMultipartUploadNonAlignedFinalPart`** - Tests irregular final part sizes\n- **Content verification** - Byte-for-byte comparison with mismatch debugging\n\n### HTTP Handler Tests (`/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/handlers/multipart_routing_test.go`):\n- **`TestUploadPartRoutingNeverFallsThroughToPut`** - ADR-002 regression tripwire\n- **`TestMultipartFullCycleByteVerification`** - Full HTTP path testing (45MB, 3 parts)\n- **`TestMultipartSuspectPatterns`** - Tests rejection of unsupported patterns:\n  - U6: Out-of-order/parallel parts\n  - U7: Part retry after network failure  \n  - U8: Non-block-aligned intermediate parts\n\n### Canary Tests (`/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/canary/canary_test.go`):\n- **`TestMonitorCheck`** - Single canary verification\n- **`TestMonitorMultipartCheck`** - Multipart canary with 100-byte test data\n- **`TestMonitorMultipartIntegration`** - Full multipart check with metrics verification\n- **Mock backend** with functional multipart operations (lines 232-316)\n\n## 5. Origin from bf-3wm1me\n\nThe multipart upload primitives were added in the **bf-3wm1me** branch, which fixed critical bugs in the helper functions:\n- Fixed `UploadPart()` passing nil instead of data reader\n- Fixed `UploadPartsInSeries()` passing nil instead of part data readers\n- Added comprehensive documentation and test coverage\n\n## Key Testing Patterns\n\n1. **Distinguishable content patterns** - Each part gets unique byte patterns for verification\n2. **Byte-for-byte verification** - Download and compare exact uploaded content\n3. **Part boundary testing** - Verify encryption/decryption across part boundaries\n4. **Error injection** - Test failure modes and cleanup\n5. **Routing validation** - Ensure HTTP requests reach correct backend methods\n6. **Size verification** - Check Content-Length reflects plaintext size, not ciphertext\n\nThis implementation provides a robust foundation for canary monitors to exercise and verify the entire multipart upload pipeline with comprehensive integrity checks."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_0c6afec014874b87b87bbcdc","session_id":"4af9d6d5-75e4-43f9-bf14-ccb629368a25","uuid":"0febe823-336a-4651-8919-afa8cb3a726e","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find canary monitor and multipart code"}

error\n241\t\n242\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryChecks()\n243\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\n244\t\n245\t\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\n246\t\t\tif attempt > 0 {\n247\t\t\t\tselect {\n248\t\t\t\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\n249\t\t\t\tcase check performs a single canary integrity check.\n271\tfunc (m *Monitor) check(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n272\t\tresult := &Result{\n273\t\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n274\t\t}\n275\t\n276\t\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n277\t\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n278\t\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.canarySize)\n279\t\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n280\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate canary content: %w\", err)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n284\t\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n285\t\n286\t\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n287\t\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n288\t\n289\t\t// Generate DEK and IV\n290\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n291\t\tif err != nil {\n292\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n293\t\t}\n294\t\n295\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n296\t\tif err != nil {\n297\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n298\t\t}\n299\t\n300\t\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n301\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n302\t\tif err != nil {\n303\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n304\t\t}\n305\t\n306\t\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n307\t\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n308\t\n309\t\t// Create envelope header\n310\t\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n311\t\tif err != nil {\n312\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n313\t\t}\n314\t\n315\t\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n316\t\tif err != nil {\n317\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n318\t\t}\n319\t\n320\t\t// Encrypt\n321\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n322\t\tif err != nil {\n323\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n324\t\t}\n325\t\n326\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n327\t\tif err != nil {\n328\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n329\t\t}\n330\t\n331\t\t// Build envelope\n332\t\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n333\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n334\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n335\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n336\t\n337\t\t// Compute ETag\n338\t\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n339\t\n340\t\t// Build metadata\n341\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n342\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n343\t\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n344\t\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n345\t\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n346\t\t\tIV:            iv,\n347\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n348\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n349\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n350\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n351\t\n352\t\t// Upload to B2 (direct, not through Cloudflare)\n353\t\tuploadStart := time.Now()\n354\t\tif err := m.backend.Put(ctx, m.bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\n355\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload canary: %w\", err)\n356\t\t}\n357\t\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\n358\t\n359\t\t// Download through Cloudflare (via GetRangeWithHeaders to capture CF-Cache-Status)\n360\t\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\n361\t\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\n362\t\tif err != nil {\n363\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to download canary: %w\", err)\n364\t\t}\n365\t\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n366\t\tbody.Close()\n367\t\tif err != nil {\n368\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read downloaded canary: %w\", err)\n369\t\t}\n370\t\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\n371\t\n372\t\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\n373\t\t// CF-Cache-Status values: HIT, MISS, EXPIRED, STALE, BYPASS, REVALIDATED, UPDATING, IGNORED\n374\t\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\"CF-Cache-Status\"]; ok {\n375\t\t\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \"HIT\" || cfStatus == \"STALE\" || cfStatus == \"REVALIDATED\")\n376\t\t}\n377\t\n378\t\t// Parse header\n379\t\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\n380\t\tif err != nil {\n381\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decode header: %w\", err)\n382\t\t}\n383\t\n384\t\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\n385\t\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\n386\t\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\n387\t\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\n388\t\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\n389\t\n390\t\tif hmacEnd > int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\n391\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\")\n392\t\t}\n393\t\n394\t\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\n395\t\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\n396\t\n397\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n398\t\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\n399\t\tif err != nil {\n400\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\", err)\n401\t\t}\n402\t\n403\t\t// Create decryptor\n404\t\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, m.blockSize)\n405\t\tif err != nil {\n406\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create decryptor: %w\", err)\n407\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\t// Verify HMACs\n410\t\tif err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\n411\t\t\tresult.HMACVerified = false\n412\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC verification failed: %w\", err)\n413\t\t}\n414\t\tresult.HMACVerified = true\n415\t\n416\t\t// Decrypt\n417\t\tdecrypted, err := decryptor.Decrypt(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC)\n418\t\tif err != nil {\n419\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decrypt: %w\", err)\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\t// Verify decrypted content matches original\n423\t\tif !bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n424\t\t\tresult.DecryptVerified = false\n425\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"decrypted content does not match original\")\n426\t\t}\n427\t\tresult.DecryptVerified = true\n428\t\n429\t\t// Verify plaintext SHA\n430\t\tif err := downloadedHeader.VerifyPlaintextSHA(decrypted); err != nil {\n431\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA verification failed: %w\", err)\n432\t\t}\n433\t\n434\t\t// Clean up canary (best effort)\n435\t\tgo func() {\n436\t\t\tcleanupCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)\n437\t\t\tdefer cancel()\n438\t\t\tm.backend.Delete(cleanupCtx, m.bucket, key)\n439\t\t}()\n440\t\n441\t\tresult.Status = StatusHealthy\n442\t\n443\t\treturn result, nil\n444\t}\n445\t\n446\t// checkMultipart performs a multipart canary check to exercise the multipart upload path.\n447\tfunc (m *Monitor) checkMultipart(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n448\t\tresult := &Result{\n449\t\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n450\t\t}\n451\t\n452\t\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n453\t\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n454\t\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.multipartSize)\n455\t\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n456\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate multipart canary content: %w\", err)\n457\t\t}\n458\t\n459\t\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n460\t\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n461\t\n462\t\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n463\t\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary-multipart/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n464\t\n465\t\t// Generate DEK and IV\n466\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n467\t\tif err != nil {\n468\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n469\t\t}\n470\t\n471\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n472\t\tif err != nil {\n473\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n474\t\t}\n475\t\n476\t\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n477\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n478\t\tif err != nil {\n479\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n480\t\t}\n481\t\n482\t\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n483\t\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n484\t\n485\t\t// Create envelope header\n486\t\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n487\t\tif err != nil {\n488\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n489\t\t}\n490\t\n491\t\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n492\t\tif err != nil {\n493\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n494\t\t}\n495\t\n496\t\t// Encrypt\n497\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n498\t\tif err != nil {\n499\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n500\t\t}\n501\t\n502\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n503\t\tif err != nil {\n504\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n505\t\t}\n506\t\n507\t\t// Build envelope\n508\t\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n509\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n510\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n511\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n512\t\n513\t\t// Compute ETag\n514\t\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n515\t\n516\t\t// Build metadata\n517\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n518\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n519\t\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n520\t\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n521\t\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n522\t\t\tIV:            iv,\n523\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n524\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n525\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n526\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n527\t\n528\t\t// Upload via multipart API (exercises create/upload/complete path)\n529\t\tuploadStart := time.Now()\n530\t\n531\t\t// Step 1: Create multipart upload\n532\t\tuploadID, err := m.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, meta)\n533\t\tif err != nil {\n534\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create multipart upload: %w\", err)\n535\t\t}\n536\t\n537\t\t// Step 2: Upload parts (split into 2MB parts for reasonable part count)\n538\t\tconst partSize = 2 * 1024 * 1024 // 2MB parts\n539\t\tvar parts []backend.CompletedPart\n540\t\tpartNum := int32(1)\n541\t\n542\t\tfor offset := 0; offset < len(envelope); offset += partSize {\n543\t\t\tend := offset + partSize\n544\t\t\tif end > len(envelope) {\n545\t\t\t\tend = len(envelope)\n546\t\t\t}\n547\t\n548\t\t\tpartData := envelope[offset:end]\n549\t\t\tpartETag, err := m.backend.UploadPart(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, partNum, bytes.NewReader(partData), int64(len(partData)))\n550\t\t\tif err != nil {\n551\t\t\t\t// Abort on failure\n552\t\t\t\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\n553\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload part %d: %w\", partNum, err)\n554\t\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t\tparts = append(parts, backend.CompletedPart{\n557\t\t\t\tPartNumber: partNum,\n558\t\t\t\tETag:       partETag,\n559\t\t\t})\n560\t\n561\t\t\tpartNum++\n562\t\t}\n563\t\n564\t\t// Step 3: Complete multipart upload\n565\t\t_, err = m.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n566\t\tif err != nil {\n567\t\t\t// Abort on failure\n568\t\t\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\n569\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to complete multipart upload: %w\", err)\n570\t\t}\n571\t\n572\t\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\n573\t\n574\t\t// Download and verify (same verification as regular canary)\n575\t\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\n576\t\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\n577\t\tif err != nil {\n578\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to download multipart canary: %w\", len(envelope) {\n594\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"multipart download size mismatch: got %d, expected %d\", len(downloadedEnvelope), len(envelope))\n595\t\t}\n596\t\n597\t\t// Byte-for-byte failed\")\n600\t\t}\n601\t\n602\t\t// Parse header\n603\t\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\n604\t\tif err != nil {\n605\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decode int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\n615\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\")\n616\t\t}\n617\t\n618\t\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\n619\t\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\n620\t\n621\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n622\t\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\n623\t\tif err != nil {\n624\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\", err)\n625\t\t}\n626\t\n627\t\t// Create decryptor\n628\t\tdecryptor, err := err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\n635\t\t\tresult.HMACVerified = false\n636\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC verification failed: %w\", nil\n669\t}\n670\t\n671\t// updateStateSuccess updates state after a successful check.\n672\tfunc (m *Monitor) updateStateSuccess(result *Result) {\n673\t\tm.state.mu.Lock()\n674\t\tdefer Cloudflare, and verifying the decryption.\npackage canary\n\nimport `json:\"download_latency_ms\"`\n\tDecryptVerified   bool      `json:\"decrypt_verified\"`\n\tHMACVerified      bool      `json:\"hmac_verified\"`\n\tCFCacheHit        bool       string    `json:\"multipart_last_error,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Monitor manages the canary integrity checks.\ntype Monitor struct {\n\tbackend    backend.Backend\n\tbucket     string\n\tmek        []byte\n\tblockSize  int\n\tinstanceID string\n\n\tstate CanaryState\n\n\t// Configuration\n\tinterval          time.Duration\n\tcanarySize        int\n\tmaxRetries        int\n\tretryDelay // Size of multipart canary (default 6MB)\n}\n\n// NewMonitor creates a new canary monitor.\nfunc NewMonitor(cfg Config) *Monitor {\n\tif cfg.Interval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.Interval = 5 * time.Minute\n\t}\n\tif cfg.CanarySize == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.CanarySize = 1024\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MaxRetries == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MaxRetries = 3\n\t}\n\tif cfg.RetryDelay == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.RetryDelay = 10 * time.Second\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MultipartInterval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MultipartInterval = 1 * time.Hour\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MultipartSize == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MultipartSize = 6 * 1024 * 1024 // 6MB (above 5MB threshold)\n\t}\n\n\tinstanceID := cfg.InstanceID\n\tif instanceID == \"\" {\n\t\tinstanceID, _ = os.Hostname()\n\t\tif instanceID == \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Generate random ID\n\t\t\tb := make([]byte, 8)\n\t\t\trand.Read(b)\n\t\t\tinstanceID = hex.EncodeToString(b)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Monitor{\n\t\tbackend:           cfg.Backend,\n\t\tbucket:            cfg.Bucket,\n\t\tmek:               cfg.MEK,\n\t\tblockSize:         cfg.BlockSize,\n\t\tinstanceID:        instanceID,\n\t\tinterval:          cfg.Interval,\n\t\tcanarySize:        cfg.CanarySize,\n\t\tmaxRetries:        cfg.MaxRetries,\n\t\tretryDelay:        cfg.RetryDelay,\n\t\tmultipartInterval: cfg.MultipartInterval,\n\t\tmultipartSize:     cfg.MultipartSize,\n\t\tstopCh:            make(chan struct{}),\n\t\tdoneCh:            make(chan struct{}),\n\t\tstate: CanaryState{\n\t\t\tStatus:           StatusUnknown,\n\t\t\tMultipartHealthy: StatusUnknown,\n\t\t\tInstanceID:       instanceID,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// Start begins the periodic canary checks.\n// It runs an initial check immediately, then periodically.\nfunc (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) {\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer close(m.doneCh)\n\n\t\t// Initial checks\n\t\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\t\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\n\n\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(m.interval)\n\t\tmultipartTicker := time.NewTicker(m.multipartInterval)\n\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\t\tdefer multipartTicker.Stop()\n\n\t\tfor {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-m.stopCh:\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\t\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\t\t\tcase <-multipartTicker.C:\n\t\t\t\tm.runMultipartCheck(ctx)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n}\n\n// Stop stops the canary monitor.\nfunc (m *Monitor) Stop() {\n\tclose(m.stopCh)\n\t<-m.doneCh\n}\n\n// runCheck performs a single canary check with retries.\nfunc (m *Monitor) runCheck(ctx context.Context) {\n\tvar lastErr error\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryChecks()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\n\n\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\n\t\tif attempt > 0 {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult, err := m.check(ctx)\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tm.updateStateSuccess(result)\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(\"\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tlastErr = err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncCanaryFailures()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\n\tm.updateStateFailure(lastErr)\n}\n\n// runMultipartCheck performs a single multipart canary check with retries.\nfunc (m *Monitor) runMultipartCheck(ctx context.Context) {\n\tvar lastErr error\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryChecks()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastCheck(time.Now())\n\n\tfor attempt := 0; attempt < m.maxRetries; attempt++ {\n\t\tif attempt > 0 {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(m.retryDelay):\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult, err := m.checkMultipart(ctx)\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tm.updateMultipartStateSuccess(result)\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(\"\")\n\t\t\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(true)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tlastErr = err\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryFailures()\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError(lastErr.Error())\n\tmetrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy(false)\n\tm.updateMultipartStateFailure(lastErr)\n}\n\n// check performs a single canary integrity check.\nfunc (m *Monitor) check(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n\tresult := &Result{\n\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.canarySize)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate canary content: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n\n\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Upload to B2 (direct, not through Cloudflare)\n\tuploadStart := time.Now()\n\tif err := m.backend.Put(ctx, m.bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Download through Cloudflare (via GetRangeWithHeaders to capture CF-Cache-Status)\n\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\n\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to download canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tbody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read downloaded canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\n\t// CF-Cache-Status values: HIT, MISS, EXPIRED, STALE, BYPASS, REVALIDATED, UPDATING, IGNORED\n\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\"CF-Cache-Status\"]; ok {\n\t\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \"HIT\" || cfStatus == \"STALE\" || cfStatus == \"REVALIDATED\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse header\n\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\n\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\n\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\n\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\n\n\tif hmacEnd > int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\")\n\t}\n\n\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\n\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create decryptor\n\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create decryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify HMACs\n\tif err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\n\t\tresult.HMACVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.HMACVerified = true\n\n\t// Decrypt\n\tdecrypted, err := decryptor.Decrypt(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify decrypted content matches original\n\tif !bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\tresult.DecryptVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"decrypted content does not match original\")\n\t}\n\tresult.DecryptVerified = true\n\n\t// Verify plaintext SHA\n\tif err := downloadedHeader.VerifyPlaintextSHA(decrypted); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up canary (best effort)\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tcleanupCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t\tm.backend.Delete(cleanupCtx, m.bucket, key)\n\t}()\n\n\tresult.Status = StatusHealthy\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// checkMultipart performs a multipart canary check to exercise the multipart upload path.\nfunc (m *Monitor) checkMultipart(ctx context.Context) (*Result, error) {\n\tresult := &Result{\n\t\tLastCheck: time.Now(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate unique canary content with timestamp\n\ttimestamp := time.Now().UnixNano()\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, m.multipartSize)\n\tif _, err := rand.Read(canaryContent); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate multipart canary content: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Embed timestamp for verification\n\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(canaryContent[:8], uint64(timestamp))\n\n\t// Generate unique key for this canary\n\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\".armor/canary-multipart/%s/%d\", m.instanceID, timestamp)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to generate IV: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(m.mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Create envelope header\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), m.blockSize, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create encryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     m.blockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Upload via multipart API (exercises create/upload/complete path)\n\tuploadStart := time.Now()\n\n\t// Step 1: Create multipart upload\n\tuploadID, err := m.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 2: Upload parts (split into 2MB parts for reasonable part count)\n\tconst partSize = 2 * 1024 * 1024 // 2MB parts\n\tvar parts []backend.CompletedPart\n\tpartNum := int32(1)\n\n\tfor offset := 0; offset < len(envelope); offset += partSize {\n\t\tend := offset + partSize\n\t\tif end > len(envelope) {\n\t\t\tend = len(envelope)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tpartData := envelope[offset:end]\n\t\tpartETag, err := m.backend.UploadPart(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, partNum, bytes.NewReader(partData), int64(len(partData)))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// Abort on failure\n\t\t\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload part %d: %w\", partNum, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tparts = append(parts, backend.CompletedPart{\n\t\t\tPartNumber: partNum,\n\t\t\tETag:       partETag,\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tpartNum++\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 3: Complete multipart upload\n\t_, err = m.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Abort on failure\n\t\tm.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, m.bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to complete multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tresult.UploadLatencyMs = time.Since(uploadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Download and verify (same verification as regular canary)\n\tdownloadStart := time.Now()\n\tbody, headers, err := m.backend.GetRangeWithHeaders(ctx, m.bucket, key, 0, int64(len(envelope)))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to download multipart canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdownloadedEnvelope, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tbody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read downloaded multipart canary: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.DownloadLatencyMs = time.Since(downloadStart).Milliseconds()\n\n\t// Check Cloudflare cache status\n\tif cfStatus, ok := headers[\"CF-Cache-Status\"]; ok {\n\t\tresult.CFCacheHit = (cfStatus == \"HIT\" || cfStatus == \"STALE\" || cfStatus == \"REVALIDATED\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify size matches\n\tif len(downloadedEnvelope) != len(envelope) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"multipart download size mismatch: got %d, expected %d\", len(downloadedEnvelope), len(envelope))\n\t}\n\n\t// Byte-for-byte verification (critical for multipart integrity)\n\tif !bytes.Equal(downloadedEnvelope, envelope) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"multipart content byte-for-byte verification failed\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse header\n\tdownloadedHeader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(downloadedEnvelope)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decode header: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\n\tdataStart := int64(crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tdataEnd := dataStart + int64(len(canaryContent))\n\thmacStart := downloadedHeader.HMACTableOffset()\n\thmacEnd := hmacStart + int64(downloadedHeader.BlockCount())*crypto.HMACSize\n\n\tif hmacEnd > int64(len(downloadedEnvelope)) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"downloaded envelope too short for HMAC table\")\n\t}\n\n\tdownloadedEncrypted := downloadedEnvelope[dataStart:dataEnd]\n\tdownloadedHMAC := downloadedEnvelope[hmacStart:hmacEnd]\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(m.mek, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to unwrap DEK: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create decryptor\n\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, m.blockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create decryptor: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify HMACs\n\tif err := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC); err != nil {\n\t\tresult.HMACVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tresult.HMACVerified = true\n\n\t// Decrypt\n\tdecrypted, err := decryptor.Decrypt(downloadedEncrypted, downloadedHMAC)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to decrypt: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify decrypted content matches original\n\tif !bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\tresult.DecryptVerified = false\n\t\treturn nil, {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up canary (best effort)\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tcleanupCtx, cancel := StatusHealthy\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// updateStateSuccess updates state after a successful check.\nfunc (m *Monitor) updateStateSuccess(result *Result) {\n\tm.state.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.state.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tm.state.Status = StatusHealthy\n\tm.state.LastCheck = result.LastCheck\n\tm.state.LastSuccess = result.LastCheck\n\tm.state.ConsecutiveSuccess++\n\tm.state.ConsecutiveFailures = 0\n\tm.state.LastError = \"\"\n\tm.state.UploadLatencyMs = result.UploadLatencyMs\n\tm.state.DownloadLatencyMs = result.DownloadLatencyMs\n\tm.state.DecryptVerified = result.DecryptVerified\n\tm.state.HMACVerified = result.HMACVerified\n\tm.state.CFCacheHit = result.CFCacheHit\n}\n\n// updateStateFailure updates state after a failed check.\nfunc (m *Monitor) updateStateFailure(err error) {\n\tm.state.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.state.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tm.state.Status = StatusUnhealthy\n\tm.state.LastCheck = time.Now()\n\tm.state.ConsecutiveSuccess = 0\n\tm.state.ConsecutiveFailures++\n\tm.state.LastError = err.Error()\n}\n\n// updateMultipartStateSuccess updates multipart state after a successful check.\nfunc (m *Monitor) updateMultipartStateSuccess(result *Result) {\n\tm.state.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.state.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tm.state.MultipartHealthy = StatusHealthy\n\tm.state.MultipartLastCheck = result.LastCheck\n\tm.state.MultipartLastSuccess = result.LastCheck\n\tm.state.MultipartConsecutiveFails = 0\n\tm.state.MultipartLastError = \"\"\n}\n\n// updateMultipartStateFailure updates multipart state after a failed check.\nfunc (m *Monitor) updateMultipartStateFailure(err error) {\n\tm.state.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer m.state.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tm.state.MultipartHealthy = StatusUnhealthy\n\tm.state.MultipartLastCheck = time.Now()\n\tm.state.MultipartConsecutiveFails++\n\tm.state.MultipartLastError = err.Error()\n}\n\n// GetStatus returns the current canary status.\nfunc (m *Monitor) GetStatus() Result {\n\tm.state.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer m.state.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\treturn Result{\n\t\tStatus:                    m.state.Status,\n\t\tLastCheck:                 m.state.LastCheck,\n\t\tUploadLatencyMs:           m.state.UploadLatencyMs,\n\t\tDownloadLatencyMs:         m.state.DownloadLatencyMs,\n\t\tDecryptVerified:           m.state.DecryptVerified,\n\t\tHMACVerified:              m.state.HMACVerified,\n\t\tCFCacheHit:                m.state.CFCacheHit,\n\t\tLastError:                 m.state.LastError,\n\t\tMultipartHealthy:          m.state.MultipartHealthy,\n\t\tMultipartLastCheck:        m.state.MultipartLastCheck,\n\t\tMultipartConsecutiveFails: m.state.MultipartConsecutiveFails,\n\t\tMultipartLastError:        m.state.MultipartLastError,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsHealthy returns true if the canary is healthy.\nfunc (m *Monitor) IsHealthy() bool {\n\tm.state.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer m.state.mu.RUnlock()\n\treturn m.state.Status == StatusHealthy\n}\n\n// MarshalJSON returns the state as JSON.\nfunc (m *Monitor) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\n\tm.state.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer m.state.mu.RUnlock()\n\treturn json.Marshal(&m.state)\n}\n","numLines":758,"startLine":1,"totalLines":758}}}

context.Context, info *MultipartUploadInfo) (string, error) {\n\tif len(info.Parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"cannot complete upload with no parts\")\n\t}\n\n\tetag, err := h.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, h.bucket, info.Key, info.UploadID, info.Parts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to complete multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn etag, nil\n}\n\n// AbortUpload cancels the multipart upload and cleans up any uploaded parts.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) AbortUpload(ctx context.Context, info *MultipartUploadInfo) error {\n\terr := h.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, h.bucket, info.Key, info.UploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to abort multipart upload: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// UploadPartsInSeries uploads multiple parts in series (one after another).\n// This is simpler than parallel uploads but slower for large files.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) UploadPartsInSeries(ctx context.Context, info *MultipartUploadInfo, partSize int, data []byte) error {\n\tif len(data) == 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no data to upload\")\n\t}\n\n\tpartNumber := 1\n\toffset := 0\n\n\tfor offset < len(data) {\n\t\tend := offset + partSize\n\t\tif end > len(data) {\n\t\t\tend = len(data)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tpartData := data[offset:end]\n\t\tpartDataReader := io.NopCloser(bytes.NewReader(partData))\n\t\tetag, err := h.backend.UploadPart(ctx, h.bucket, info.Key, info.UploadID, int32(partNumber), partDataReader, int64(len(partData)))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to upload part %d: %w\", partNumber, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tinfo.Parts = append(info.Parts, CompletedPart{\n\t\t\tPartNumber: int32(partNumber),\n\t\t\tETag:       etag,\n\t\t})\n\n\t\toffset = end\n\t\tpartNumber++\n\t}\n\n\tinfo.Size = int64(len(data))\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// CanaryUpload performs a simple canary test of the multipart upload system.\n// It uploads a small amount of data using multipart and verifies the result.\nfunc (h *MultipartUploadHelper) CanaryUpload(ctx context.Context, key string, data []byte) error {\n\t// Create upload\n\tinfo, err := h.CreateUpload(ctx, key, map[string]string{\n\t\t\"Content-Type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\t\"canary-test\": \"true\",\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary create failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Upload as a single part (simplest case for canary)\n\terr = h.UploadPartsInSeries(ctx, info, len(data), data)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Try to clean up\n\t\t_ = h.AbortUpload(ctx, info)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary upload failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Complete upload\n\t_, err = h.CompleteUpload(ctx, info)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Try to clean up\n\t\t_ = h.AbortUpload(ctx, info)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary complete failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify the object exists\n\t_, objInfo, err := h.backend.Get(ctx, h.bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary verification failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif objInfo.Size != int64(len(data)) {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary verification failed: size mismatch (got %d, expected %d)\", objInfo.Size, len(data))\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up\n\terr = h.backend.Delete(ctx, h.bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"canary cleanup failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// VerifyMultipartUpload uploads data via multipart and downloads it back to verify integrity.\n// This is useful for canary monitors to verify the full multipart upload code path.\nfunc VerifyMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, backend Backend, bucket, key string, data []byte, partSize int) error {\n\tif len(data) < 5*1024*1024 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"data size must be at least 5MB for multipart upload\")\n\t}\n\n\tif partSize < 5*1024*1024 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"part size must be at least 5MB\")\n\t}\n\n\thelper := NewMultipartUploadHelper(backend, bucket)\n\n\t// Create multipart upload\n\tinfo, err := helper.CreateUpload(ctx, key, map[string]string{\n\t\t\"Content-Type\":      \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\t\"x-amz-meta-test\":   \"multipart-verification\",\n\t\t\"x-amz-meta-size\":   fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", len(data)),\n\t\t\"x-amz-meta-source\": \"canary-monitor\",\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"create failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Upload all parts\n\terr = helper.UploadPartsInSeries(ctx, info, partSize, data)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t_ = helper.AbortUpload(ctx, info)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"upload parts failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Complete upload\n\t_, err = helper.CompleteUpload(ctx, info)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t_ = helper.AbortUpload(ctx, info)\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"complete failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Download and verify\n\tbody, objInfo, err := backend.Get(ctx, bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"get failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer body.Close()\n\n\t// Verify size matches before reading the full body\n\tif objInfo.Size != int64(len(data)) {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"size mismatch: got %d bytes, expected %d\", objInfo.Size, len(data))\n\t}\n\n\tdownloadedData, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"read download failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(downloadedData) != len(data) {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"size mismatch: downloaded %d bytes, expected %d\", len(downloadedData), len(data))\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up\n\terr = backend.Delete(ctx, bucket, key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"cleanup failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// MultipartUploadConstants defines important constants for multipart uploads.\ntype MultipartUploadConstants struct {\n\t// Minimum part size for multipart uploads (5MB)\n\tMinPartSize int64\n\n\t// Maximum part size for multipart uploads (5GB)\n\tMaxPartSize int64\n\n\t// Maximum number of parts per upload\n\tMaxPartsCount int\n\n\t// Minimum object size that should use multipart upload (5MB)\n\tMinMultipartObjectSize int64\n\n\t// Maximum object size achievable via multipart upload (5TB)\n\tMaxObjectSize int64\n}\n\n// GetMultipartUploadConstants returns the standard S3 multipart upload constants.\nfunc GetMultipartUploadConstants() MultipartUploadConstants {\n\tconst (\n\t\tminPartSize            = 5 * 1024 * 1024            // 5MB\n\t\tmaxPartSize            = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024     // 5GB\n\t\tmaxPartsCount          = 10000\n\t\tminMultipartObjectSize = 5 * 1024 * 1024            // 5MB\n\t\tmaxObjectSize          = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 5TB\n\t)\n\n\treturn MultipartUploadConstants{\n\t\tMinPartSize:            minPartSize,\n\t\tMaxPartSize:            maxPartSize,\n\t\tMaxPartsCount:         maxPartsCount,\n\t\tMinMultipartObjectSize: minMultipartObjectSize,\n\t\tMaxObjectSize:          maxObjectSize,\n\t}\n}\n\n// CalculateOptimalPartSize calculates the optimal part size for a given file size.\n// It aims to use parts between 10MB and 100MB for efficiency while staying within limits.\nfunc CalculateOptimalPartSize(fileSize int64) int64 {\n\tconst (\n\t\tminPartSize = 10 * 1024 * 1024  // 10MB\n\t\tmaxPartSize = 100 * 1024 * 1024 // 100MB\n\t\tmaxParts    = 10000\n\t)\n\n\t// For small files, use the minimum part size\n\tif fileSize <= minPartSize*maxParts {\n\t\treturn minPartSize\n\t}\n\n\t// For larger files, calculate a part size that keeps us under maxParts\n\tpartSize := fileSize / maxParts\n\tif partSize < minPartSize {\n\t\tpartSize = minPartSize\n\t}\n\tif partSize > maxPartSize {\n\t\tpartSize = maxPartSize\n\t}\n\n\treturn partSize\n}\n","numLines":314,"startLine":1,"totalLines":314}}}

map[string]string, error) {\n72\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n73\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n74\t\n75\t\tk := bucket + \"/\" + key\n76\t\tdata, ok := m.objects[k]\n77\t\tif !ok {\n78\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"object not found: %s\", key)\n79\t\t}\n80\t\tif offset >= int64(len(data)) {\n81\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"offset out of range\")\n82\t\t}\n83\t\tend := offset + length\n84\t\tif end > int64(len(data)) {\n85\t\t\tend = int64(len(data))\n86\t\t}\n87\t\t// Mock doesn't simulate CF caching, so return empty headers\n88\t\treturn io.NopCloser(bytes.NewReader(data[offset:end])), make(map[string]string), nil\n89\t}\n90\t\n91\tfunc (m *mockBackend) Head(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (*backend.ObjectInfo, error) {\n92\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n93\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n94\t\n95\t\tk := bucket + \"/\" + key\n96\t\tdata, ok := m.objects[k]\n97\t\tif !ok {\n98\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"object not found: %s\", key)\n99\t\t}\n100\t\treturn &backend.ObjectInfo{\n101\t\t\tKey:      key,\n102\t\t\tSize:     int64(len(data)),\n103\t\t\tMetadata: m.meta[k],\n104\t\t}, nil\n105\t}\n106\t\n107\tfunc (m *mockBackend) Delete(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) error {\n108\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n109\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n110\t\n111\t\tk := bucket + \"/\" + key\n112\t\tdelete(m.objects, k)\n113\t\tdelete(m.meta, k)\n114\t\treturn nil\n115\t}\n116\t\n117\tfunc (m *mockBackend) DeleteObjects(ctx context.Context, bucket string, keys []string) error {\n118\t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n119\t\t\tm.Delete(ctx, bucket, key)\n120\t\t}\n121\t\treturn nil\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\tfunc (m *mockBackend) List(ctx context.Context, bucket, prefix, delimiter, continuationToken string, maxKeys int) (*backend.ListResult, error) {\n125\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n126\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n127\t\n128\t\tvar objects []backend.ObjectInfo\n129\t\tprefix = bucket + \"/\" + prefix\n130\t\tfor k, data := range m.objects {\n131\t\t\tif len(prefix) > 0 && len(k) < len(prefix) || k[:len(prefix)] != prefix {\n132\t\t\t\tcontinue\n133\t\t\t}\n134\t\t\tkey := k[len(bucket)+1:]\n135\t\t\tobjects = append(objects, backend.ObjectInfo{\n136\t\t\t\tKey:      key,\n137\t\t\t\tSize:     int64(len(data)),\n138\t\t\t\tMetadata: m.meta[k],\n139\t\t\t})\n140\t\t}\n141\t\treturn &backend.ListResult{Objects: objects}, nil\n142\t}\n143\t\n144\tfunc (m *mockBackend) Copy(ctx context.Context, srcBucket, srcKey, dstBucket, dstKey string, meta map[string]string, replaceMetadata bool) error {\n145\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n146\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n147\t\n148\t\tsrc := srcBucket + \"/\" + srcKey\n149\t\tdst := dstBucket + \"/\" + dstKey\n150\t\tdata, ok := m.objects[src]\n151\t\tif !ok {\n152\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"source object not found: %s\", srcKey)\n153\t\t}\n154\t\tm.objects[dst] = data\n155\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n156\t\t\tm.meta[dst] = meta\n157\t\t} else {\n158\t\t\t// Copy existing metadata\n159\t\t\texistingMeta := m.meta[src]\n160\t\t\tnewMeta := make(map[string]string)\n161\t\t\tfor k, v := range existingMeta {\n162\t\t\t\tnewMeta[k] = v\n163\t\t\t}\n164\t\t\tfor k, v := range meta {\n165\t\t\t\tnewMeta[k] = v\n166\t\t\t}\n167\t\t\tm.meta[dst] = newMeta\n168\t\t}\n169\t\treturn nil\n170\t}\n171\t\n172\tfunc (m *mockBackend) ListBuckets(ctx context.Context) ([]backend.BucketInfo, error) {\n173\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n174\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n175\t\n176\t\tbuckets := make(map[string]time.Time)\n177\t\tfor k := range m.objects {\n178\t\t\tparts := strings.SplitN(k, \"/\", 2)\n179\t\t\tif len(parts) > 0 && parts[0] != \"\" {\n180\t\t\t\tbucket := parts[0]\n181\t\t\t\tif _, exists := buckets[bucket]; !exists {\n182\t\t\t\t\tbuckets[bucket] = time.Now()\n183\t\t\t\t}\n184\t\t\t}\n185\t\t}\n186\t\n187\t\tresult := make([]backend.BucketInfo, 0, len(buckets))\n188\t\tfor name, created := range buckets {\n189\t\t\tresult = append(result, backend.BucketInfo{\n190\t\t\t\tName:         name,\n191\t\t\t\tCreationDate: created,\n192\t\t\t})\n193\t\t}\n194\t\treturn result, nil\n195\t}\n196\t\n197\tfunc (m *mockBackend) CreateBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n198\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n199\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n200\t\tm.objects[bucket+\"/.bucket\"] = nil\n201\t\treturn nil\n202\t}\n203\t\n204\tfunc (m *mockBackend) DeleteBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n205\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n206\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n207\t\tfor k := range m.objects {\n208\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, bucket+\"/\") && k != bucket+\"/.bucket\" {\n209\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"bucket not empty\")\n210\t\t\t}\n211\t\t}\n212\t\tdelete(m.objects, bucket+\"/.bucket\")\n213\t\treturn nil\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\tfunc (m *mockBackend) HeadBucket(ctx context.Context, bucket string) error {\n217\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n218\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n219\t\tfor k := range m.objects {\n220\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, bucket+\"/\") {\n221\t\t\t\treturn nil\n222\t\t\t}\n223\t\t}\n224\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"bucket not found: %s\", bucket)\n225\t}\n226\t\n227\tfunc (m *mockBackend) GetDirect(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string) (io.ReadCloser, *backend.ObjectInfo, error) {\n228\t\treturn m.Get(ctx, bucket, key)\n229\t}\n230\t\n231\t// Multipart upload methods (functional implementations for testing)\n232\tfunc (m *mockBackend) CreateMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, meta map[string]string) (string, error) {\n233\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n234\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n235\t\n236\t\tuploadID := fmt.Sprintf(\"upload-%d\", time.Now().UnixNano())\n237\t\t// Initialize storage for this upload's parts\n238\t\tm.multipartData[uploadID] = make(map[int32][]byte)\n239\t\t// Store metadata for later use when completing\n240\t\tm.meta[bucket+\"/\"+key+\"__pending__\"+uploadID] = meta\n241\t\treturn uploadID, nil\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\tfunc (m *mockBackend) UploadPart(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, partNumber int32, body io.Reader, size int64) (string, error) {\n245\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n246\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n247\t\n248\t\t// Read the part data\n249\t\tdata, err := io.ReadAll(body)\n250\t\tif err != nil {\n251\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read part data: %w\", err)\n252\t\t}\n253\t\n254\t\t// Store the part data\n255\t\tif _, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]; !exists {\n256\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n257\t\t}\n258\t\tm.multipartData[uploadID][partNumber] = data\n259\t\n260\t\t// Return ETag (use hash of data for uniqueness)\n261\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"etag-%x-%d\", sha256.Sum256(data), partNumber), nil\n262\t}\n263\t\n264\tfunc (m *mockBackend) CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string, parts []backend.CompletedPart) (string, error) {\n265\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n266\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n267\t\n268\t\t// Check if upload exists\n269\t\tpartsData, exists := m.multipartData[uploadID]\n270\t\tif !exists {\n271\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"upload ID %s does not exist\", uploadID)\n272\t\t}\n273\t\n274\t\t// Combine all parts in order\n275\t\tvar combined []byte\n276\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n277\t\t\tpartData, ok := partsData[part.PartNumber]\n278\t\t\tif !ok {\n279\t\t\t\t// Clean up and return error\n280\t\t\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n281\t\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"part %d not found\", part.PartNumber)\n282\t\t\t}\n283\t\t\tcombined = append(combined, partData...)\n284\t\t}\n285\t\n286\t\t// Store the combined object\n287\t\tobjectKey := bucket + \"/\" + key\n288\t\tm.objects[objectKey] = combined\n289\t\n290\t\t// Move metadata from pending to final\n291\t\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n292\t\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n293\t\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n294\t\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n295\t\t}\n296\t\n297\t\t// Clean up multipart data\n298\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n299\t\n300\t\t// Return final ETag\n301\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\tfunc (m *mockBackend) AbortMultipartUpload(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) error {\n305\t\tm.mu.Lock()\n306\t\tdefer m.mu.Unlock()\n307\t\n308\t\t// Clean up multipart data\n309\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n310\t\n311\t\t// Clean up pending metadata\n312\t\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n313\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n314\t\n315\t\treturn nil\n316\t}\n317\t\n318\tfunc (m *mockBackend) ListParts(ctx context.Context, bucket, key, uploadID string) (*backend.ListPartsResult, error) {\n319\t\treturn &backend.ListPartsResult{}, nil\n320\t}\n321\t\n322\tfunc (m *mockBackend) ListMultipartUploads(ctx context.Context, bucket string) (*backend.ListMultipartUploadsResult, error) {\n323\t\treturn &backend.ListMultipartUploadsResult{}, nil\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t// Lifecycle configuration methods (stub implementations for testing)\n327\tfunc (m *mockBackend) GetBucketLifecycleConfiguration(ctx
