tests Monitor creation.\n373\tfunc TestNewMonitor(t *testing.T) {\n374\t\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n375\t\trand.Read(mek)\n376\t\n377\t\tcfg := Config{\n378\t\t\tBackend:    newMockBackend(),\n379\t\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n380\t\t\tMEK:        mek,\n381\t\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n382\t\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n383\t\t}\n384\t\n385\t\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n386\t\tif m == nil {\n387\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"expected monitor, got nil\")\n388\t\t}\n389\t\tif m.bucket != \"test-bucket\" {\n390\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected bucket test-bucket, got %s\", m.bucket)\n391\t\t}\n392\t\tif m.instanceID != \"test-instance\" {\n393\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected instance ID test-instance, got %s\", m.instanceID)\n394\t\t}\n395\t}\n396\t\n397\t// TestNewMonitorDefaults tests that default values are set correctly.\n398\tfunc TestNewMonitorDefaults(t *testing.T) {\n399\t\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n400\t\trand.Read(mek)\n401\t\n402\t\tcfg := Config{\n403\t\t\tBackend:   newMockBackend(),\n404\t\t\tBucket:    \"test-bucket\",\n405\t\t\tMEK:       mek,\n406\t\t\tBlockSize: 65536,\n407\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n410\t\tif {\n420\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected default retry delay 10s, got %v\", m.retryDelay)\n421\t\t}\n422\t}\n423\t\n424\t// TestMonitorCheck tests a single canary check.\n425\tfunc TestMonitorCheck(t *testing.T) {\n426\t\tmb := newMockBackend()\n427\t\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n428\t\trand.Read(mek)\n429\t\n430\t\tcfg := Config{\n431\t\t\tBackend:    mb,\n432\t\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n433\t\t\tMEK:        mek,\n434\t\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n435\t\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n436\t\t\tCanarySize: 100,\n437\t\t}\n438\t\n439\t\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n440\t\tctx := context.Background()\n441\t\n442\t\tresult, err := m.check(ctx)\n443\t\tif err != nil {\n444\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"canary check failed: %v\", err)\n445\t\t}\n446\t\n447\t\tif result.Status != StatusHealthy {\n448\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected status healthy, got %s\", result.Status)\n449\t\t}\n450\t\tif !result.DecryptVerified {\n451\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected decrypt verified to be true\")\n452\t\t}\n453\t\tif !result.HMACVerified {\n454\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected HMAC verified to be true\")\n455\t\t}\n456\t\n457\t\t// Verify canary was cleaned up (async, so wait a bit)\n458\t\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n459\t\tmb.mu.Lock()\n460\t\tcount := len(mb.objects)\n461\t\tmb.mu.Unlock()\n462\t\tif count > 0 {\n463\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected canary to be cleaned up, found %d objects\", count)\n464\t\t}\n465\t}\n466\t\n467\t// TestMonitorGetStatus tests the status retrieval.\n468\tfunc TestMonitorGetStatus(t *testing.T) {\n469\t\tmb := newMockBackend()\n470\t\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n471\t\trand.Read(mek)\n472\t\n473\t\tcfg := Config{\n474\t\t\tBackend:    mb,\n475\t\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n476\t\t\tMEK:        mek,\n477\t\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n478\t\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n479\t\t}\n480\t\n481\t\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n482\t\n483\t\t// Initial status should be unknown\n484\t\tstatus := m.GetStatus()\n485\t\tif status.Status != StatusUnknown {\n486\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected initial status unknown, got %s\", status.Status)\n487\t\t}\n488\t\n489\t\t// Run a check\n490\t\tctx := context.Background()\n491\t\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n492\t\n493\t\t// Status should now be healthy\n494\t\tstatus = m.GetStatus()\n495\t\tif status.Status != StatusHealthy {\n496\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected status healthy after check, got %s\", status.Status)\n497\t\t}\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\t// TestMonitorIsHealthy tests the IsHealthy method.\n501\tfunc TestMonitorIsHealthy(t *testing.T) {\n502\t\tmb := newMockBackend()\n503\t\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n504\t\trand.Read(mek)\n505\t\n506\t\tcfg := Config{\n507\t\t\tBackend:    mb,\n508\t\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n509\t\t\tMEK:        mek,\n510\t\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n511\t\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n512\t\t}\n513\t\n514\t\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n515\t\n516\t\t// Initially unknown, not healthy\n517\t\tif m.IsHealthy() {\n518\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected IsHealthy to be false initially\")\n519\t\t}\n520\t\n521\t\t// Run a check\n522\t\tctx := context.Background()\n523\t\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n524\t\n525\t\t// Now should be healthy\n526\t\tif !m.IsHealthy() {\n527\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected IsHealthy to be true after successful check\")\n528\t\t}\n529\t}\n530\t\n531\t// TestMonitorWrongMEK tests that using a different MEK to decrypt data fails.\n532\t// This simulates a scenario where data was encrypted with one MEK but someone\n533\t// tries to decrypt with a different MEK.\n534\tfunc TestMonitorWrongMEK(t *testing.T) {\n535\t\tmb := newMockBackend()\n536\t\n537\t\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\n538\t\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n539\t\trand.Read(correctMEK)\n540\t\n541\t\twrongMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n542\t\trand.Read(wrongMEK)\n543\t\n544\t\t// Create test content\n545\t\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, 100)\n546\t\trand.Read(canaryContent)\n547\t\n548\t\t// Generate DEK and IV\n549\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n550\t\tif err != nil {\n551\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate DEK: %v\", err)\n552\t\t}\n553\t\n554\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n555\t\tif err != nil {\n556\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate IV: %v\", err)\n557\t\t}\n558\t\n559\t\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\n560\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\n561\t\tif err != nil {\n562\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err)\n563\t\t}\n564\t\n565\t\t// Encrypt with the DEK\n566\t\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n567\t\n568\t\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), 65536, plaintextSHA)\n569\t\tif err != nil {\n570\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create header: %v\", err)\n571\t\t}\n572\t\n573\t\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n574\t\tif err != nil {\n575\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encode header: %v\", err)\n576\t\t}\n577\t\n578\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, 65536)\n579\t\tif err != nil {\n580\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create encryptor: %v\", err)\n581\t\t}\n582\t\n583\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n584\t\tif err != nil {\n585\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n586\t\t}\n587\t\n588\t\t// Build envelope\n589\t\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n590\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n591\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n592\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n593\t\n594\t\t// Compute ETag\n595\t\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n596\t\n597\t\t// Build metadata with the wrapped DEK (wrapped with CORRECT MEK)\n598\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n599\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n600\t\t\tBlockSize:     65536,\n601\t\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n602\t\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n603\t\t\tIV:            iv,\n604\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n605\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n606\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n607\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n608\t\n609\t\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\n610\t\tkey := \".armor/canary/test-instance/pre-encrypted\"\n611\t\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \"test-bucket\", key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta)\n612\t\n613\t\t// Now try to unwrap the DEK with the WRONG MEK\n614\t\t// This should fail because the wrapped DEK was encrypted with the correct MEK\n615\t\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(wrongMEK, wrappedDEK)\n616\t\tif err == nil {\n617\t\t\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\n618\t\t\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\n619\t\t\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, 65536)\n620\t\t\tif decryptorErr != nil {\n621\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryptor creation failed as expected: %v\", decryptorErr)\n622\t\t\t\treturn\n623\t\t\t}\n624\t\n625\t\t\t// Try to verify HMACs - should fail because the DEK is wrong\n626\t\t\tverifyErr := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(encrypted, partsData[part.PartNumber]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Clean up and return error\n\t\t\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"part %d not found\", metadata from pending to final\n\tpendingMetaKey := bucket + \"/\" + key + \"__pending__\" + uploadID\n\tif meta, ok := m.meta[pendingMetaKey]; ok {\n\t\tm.meta[objectKey] = meta\n\t\tdelete(m.meta, pendingMetaKey)\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart data\n\tdelete(m.multipartData, uploadID)\n\n\t// Return final ETag\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"final-etag-%x\", sha256.Sum256(combined)), nil\n}\n\nfunc (m true\")\n\t}\n\tif !result.HMACVerified {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected HMAC verified to be true\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify canary was cleaned up (async, so wait a bit)\n\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\tmb.mu.Lock()\n\tcount := len(mb.objects)\n\tmb.mu.Unlock()\n\tif count > 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected canary to be cleaned up, found %d objects\", count)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorGetStatus tests the status retrieval.\nfunc TestMonitorGetStatus(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\n\t// Initial status should be unknown\n\tstatus := m.GetStatus()\n\tif status.Status != StatusUnknown {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected initial status unknown, got %s\", status.Status)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run a check\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\n\t// Status should now be healthy\n\tstatus = m.GetStatus()\n\tif status.Status != StatusHealthy {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected status healthy after check, got %s\", status.Status)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorIsHealthy tests the IsHealthy method.\nfunc TestMonitorIsHealthy(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\n\t// Initially unknown, not healthy\n\tif m.IsHealthy() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected IsHealthy to be false initially\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Run a check\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tm.runCheck(ctx)\n\n\t// Now should be healthy\n\tif !m.IsHealthy() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected IsHealthy to be true after successful check\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorWrongMEK tests that using a different MEK to decrypt data fails.\n// This simulates a scenario where data was encrypted with one MEK but someone\n// tries to decrypt with a different MEK.\nfunc TestMonitorWrongMEK(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\n\t// Generate the correct MEK and wrong MEK\n\tcorrectMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(correctMEK)\n\n\twrongMEK := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(wrongMEK)\n\n\t// Create test content\n\tcanaryContent := make([]byte, 100)\n\trand.Read(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to generate IV: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with CORRECT MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(correctMEK, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt with the DEK\n\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(canaryContent)\n\n\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, int64(len(canaryContent)), 65536, plaintextSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encode header: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, 65536)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create encryptor: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(canaryContent)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to encrypt: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build envelope\n\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, len(headerBytes)+len(encrypted)+len(hmacTable))\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n\n\t// Compute ETag\n\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(canaryContent)\n\n\t// Build metadata with the wrapped DEK (wrapped with CORRECT MEK)\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     65536,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: int64(len(canaryContent)),\n\t\tContentType:   \"application/octet-stream\",\n\t\tIV:            iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Store in mock backend at a known location\n\tkey := \".armor/canary/test-instance/pre-encrypted\"\n\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \"test-bucket\", key, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta)\n\n\t// Now try to unwrap the DEK with the WRONG MEK\n\t// This should fail because the wrapped DEK was encrypted with the correct MEK\n\tunwrappedDEK, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(wrongMEK, wrappedDEK)\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\t// If unwrap succeeded, the unwrapped DEK should be garbage\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs with the wrong unwrapped DEK - this should fail\n\t\tdecryptor, decryptorErr := crypto.NewDecryptor(unwrappedDEK, iv, 65536)\n\t\tif decryptorErr != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryptor creation failed as expected: %v\", decryptorErr)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Try to verify HMACs - should fail because the DEK is wrong\n\t\tverifyErr := decryptor.VerifyHMACs(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\tif verifyErr == nil {\n\t\t\t// If HMAC verification somehow passed, try decryption and content check\n\t\t\tdecrypted, decryptErr := decryptor.Decrypt(encrypted, hmacTable)\n\t\t\tif decryptErr == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif bytes.Equal(decrypted, canaryContent) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected decryption with wrong MEK to fail or produce garbage, but content matched\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Content didn't match - that's the expected failure\n\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"decryption produced wrong content (expected): got %d bytes, expected %d bytes\", len(decrypted), len(canaryContent))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Unwrap failed - that's the expected behavior\n\t\tt.Logf(\"DEK unwrap failed as expected with wrong MEK: %v\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMonitorStartStop tests starting and stopping the monitor.\nfunc TestMonitorStartStop(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := make([]byte, 32)\n\trand.Read(mek)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBackend:    mb,\n\t\tBucket:     \"test-bucket\",\n\t\tMEK:        mek,\n\t\tBlockSize:  65536,\n\t\tInstanceID: \"test-instance\",\n\t\tInterval:   100 * time.Millisecond, // Fast for testing\n\t}\n\n\tm := NewMonitor(cfg)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// Start the monitor\n\tm.Start(ctx)\n\n\t// Wait for at least one check\n\ttime.Sleep(150 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t// Should be healthy now\n\tif !m.IsHealthy() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected monitor to be healthy after start\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Stop the monitor\n\tm.Stop()\n}\n\n// TestMarshalJSON tests JSON serialization.\nfunc TestMarshalJSON(t *testing.T) {\n\tmb := newMockBackend()\n\tmek := `runCheck(ctx)` - Regular canary (1024 bytes)\n   - `runMultipartCheck(ctx)` - Multipart canary (6MB)\n\n2. **Periodic checks** (lines 182-199):\n   - Regular ticker: Every 5 minutes\n   - Multipart ticker: Every 1 hour\n\n3. **Retry logic** (lines 238-268):\n   - Up to 3 retries with 10-second delay\n   - Separate retry handling for multipart checks\n   - Automatic cleanup on failure via `AbortMultipartUpload`\n\n## Verification Process\n\nThe multipart canary performs comprehensive verification:\n\n1. **Upload verification** (lines 528-572):\n   - Create multipart upload\n   - Upload 2MB parts (default 3 parts for 6MB file)\n   - Complete multipart upload\n   - Abort on any failure\n\n2. **Download verification** (lines 574-600):\n   - Download full object\n   - Check Cloudflare cache status\n   - Verify size matches\n   - **Byte-for-byte comparison** with original\n\n3. **Decryption verification** (lines 602-656):\n   - Parse envelope header\n   - Extract encrypted blocks and HMAC table\n   - Unwrap DEK with MEK\n   - Verify HMACs\n   - Decrypt content\n   - **Verify decrypted content matches original**\n   - Verify plaintext SHA-256\n\n4. **Cleanup** (lines 659-663):\n   - Asynchronous delete of canary object\n   - 30-second timeout for cleanup\n\n## Tests\n\n### Unit Tests (`internal/canary/canary_test.go`)\n\n1. **TestMonitorMultipartCheck** (lines 736-780):\n   - Tests full multipart upload/download/verify cycle\n   - Increment check counter\n- `metrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastCheck()` - Set last check timestamp\n- `metrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryLastError()` - Set error message on failure\n- `metrics.DefaultMetrics.SetMultipartCanaryHealthy()` - Set health status\n- `metrics.DefaultMetrics.IncMultipartCanaryFailures()` - Increment failure counter\n\n## Configuration Examples\n\n### Default Configuration\n\n```go\ncfg := canary.Config{\n    Backend:          backend,\n    Bucket:           \"armor-bucket\",\n    MEK:              mek,\n    BlockSize:        65536,\n    Interval:         5 * time.Minute,      // Regular canary\n    MultipartInterval: 1 * time.Hour,        // Multipart canary\n    MultipartSize:    6 * 1024 * 1024,      // 6MB\n}\n```\n\n### Custom Configuration\n\n```go\ncfg := canary.Config{\n    Backend:          backend,\n    Bucket:           \"armor-bucket\",\n    MEK:              mek,\n    BlockSize:        65536,\n    Interval:         10 * time.Minute,      // Slower regular checks\n    MultipartInterval: 30 * time.Minute,     // Faster multipart checks\n    MultipartSize:    10 * 1024 * 1024,      // 10MB canary\n    MaxRetries:       5,                     // More retries\n    RetryDelay:       15 * time.Second,     // Longer delays\n}\n```\n\n## Key Differences from Regular Canary\n\n| Feature | Regular Canary | Multipart Canary |\n|---------|---------------|------------------|\n| Size | 1024 bytes (default) | 6MB (default) |\n| Upload method | `backend.Put()` | `CreateMultipartUpload` → `UploadPart` × N → `CompleteMultipartUpload` |\n| Interval | 5 minutes | 1 hour |\n| Part size | N/A | 2MB parts |\n| Verification | Same | Same + byte-for-byte comparison |\n| Purpose | Quick health check | Exercise multipart code path |\n\n## Dependencies\n\n✅ **bf-3wm1me** -
